initiative: Kinexys Rebrand (formerly Onyx)
description: JPMorgan rebranded its blockchain business unit from Onyx to Kinexys by J.P. Morgan at the Singapore FinTech Festival. At the time of rebrand, the platform had exceeded $1.5 trillion in notional value since inception and was processing over $2 billion daily. The rebrand also announced FX settlement capabilities in USD and EUR via Kinexys Digital Payments.

initiative: JPM Coin / Kinexys Digital Payments on Canton Network
description: Digital Asset and Kinexys by J.P. Morgan announced intent to bring JPM Coin (ticker: JPMD) — the first bank-issued USD-denominated deposit token — natively to the Canton Network, a privacy-enabled blockchain designed for synchronized financial markets. The phased integration throughout 2026 focuses on issuance, transfer, and near-instant redemption of JPM Coin on Canton, and also explores J.P. Morgan Blockchain Deposit Accounts.

initiative: Kinexys Fund Flow – Tokenized Private Equity Fund
description: J.P. Morgan Private Bank, J.P. Morgan Asset Management, and Citco completed the first transaction on Kinexys Fund Flow, a new blockchain solution that records investor register and transactional data on the Kinexys Digital Assets permissioned blockchain. The solution automates capital calls and distributions via smart contracts, eliminating wire services and providing granular real-time transparency. A wider rollout to real estate, infrastructure, and private credit is planned for 2026.

initiative: Kinexys Digital Assets – Scale & Transaction Volume
description: By late 2025, Kinexys had processed over $3 trillion in total transaction volume since inception, with an average daily volume exceeding $7 billion. The platform serves 400+ institutional participants across five continents. Payment transactions on the platform grew 10x year-over-year, with support for tokenized deposits, cross-border payments, repo transactions, and FX settlement.

initiative: GS DAP Spin-Out Announcement
description: Goldman Sachs announced plans to spin out its Digital Asset Platform (GS DAP) from its Digital Assets business, aiming for it to become an industry-owned distributed technology solution subject to regulatory approvals. Tradeweb was announced as the first strategic partner for GS DAP, with a planned spin-out targeted for mid-2026. GS DAP is built on technology from Digital Asset and the Canton Network.

initiative: GS DAP Tokenized Money Market Fund Solution with BNY
description: Goldman Sachs and BNY launched a collaborative tokenized money market fund (MMF) solution, the first in the U.S. allowing subscription of MMF shares via BNY's LiquidityDirect platform with corresponding mirror tokens created on GS DAP. Participating funds included BlackRock, BNY Investments Dreyfus, Federated Hermes, Fidelity Investments, and Goldman Sachs Asset Management. Mirror tokens are intended to unlock MMF shares as collateral.

initiative: Three Tokenization Projects Launch Plan
description: Goldman Sachs' global head of digital assets Mathew McDermott announced plans to launch three new tokenization projects by end of 2024, focusing on the U.S. fund complex and debt issuance in Europe. Previous work included tokenized bonds with the European Investment Bank and sovereign green bonds for the Hong Kong Monetary Authority. The bank also signaled intent to create marketplaces for tokenized assets and expand collateral use.

initiative: Citi Token Services – Euro Integration and Dublin Expansion
description: Citi expanded Citi Token Services (CTS) with Euro transaction integration and an extended footprint to Dublin, Ireland. Clients can now transfer USD and EUR 24/7 to accounts at Citi branches globally that are live on CTS. This expansion builds on CTS's existing coverage in the US, UK, Singapore, and Hong Kong, and leverages a private permissioned blockchain for instant, round-the-clock cross-border liquidity and payments.

initiative: Citi and SDX – Tokenized Private Market Assets
description: Citi joined SIX Digital Exchange (SDX) as a custodian and tokenization agent to deliver an industry-first solution for tokenizing, settling, and safekeeping late-stage pre-IPO equities on SDX's DLT-based Central Securities Depositary (CSD) platform. The collaboration targets institutional and eligible investors in high-growth venture-backed companies. The solution leverages SDX's FINMA-regulated blockchain infrastructure.

initiative: Citi Crypto Custody Launch (2026)
description: Citi announced plans to launch institutional crypto custody services in 2026, targeting asset managers and institutional clients. The bank is building its CIDAP (Citi Digital Asset Platform) as an enterprise-level bridge between traditional applications and blockchain networks, including key management infrastructure, node hosting, and digital asset wallet capabilities. Citi also partnered with Coinbase on digital asset payment capabilities for institutional clients.

initiative: HSBC Orion – UK Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) Pilot
description: HSBC Orion was selected by HM Treasury as the platform provider for the UK government's Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot issuance, the first attempt to issue tokenized sovereign bonds on a blockchain by a G7 nation. The DIGIT pilot operates within the UK's Digital Securities Sandbox (DSS) and aims for digitally native, short-dated, on-chain-settled gilts. HSBC Orion has to date enabled over $3.5 billion in digitally native bond issuances globally.

initiative: HSBC Orion – Hong Kong Government $1.3 Billion Digital Green Bond
description: HSBC Orion facilitated the world's largest digital bond issuance to date: the Hong Kong government's multi-currency $1.3 billion-equivalent green bond. Over 200 investors participated in this record-breaking issuance. HSBC also facilitated the world's first multi-currency digital green bond for Hong Kong in 2024 and the EIB's first digital sterling bond in 2023.

initiative: HSBC Tokenised Deposit Service – US and UAE Expansion
description: HSBC announced it will offer its Tokenised Deposit Service to corporate clients in the US and United Arab Emirates in the first half of 2026. The service allows clients to send money locally and cross-border in seconds around the clock, rather than only during banking hours. HSBC has already built multi-currency payment coverage in commercial bank money between global locations through this service.

initiative: HSBC Gold Token
description: HSBC became the first bank in the world to offer tokenized ownership of physical gold to retail clients, launching HSBC Gold Token in Hong Kong. The product is among the largest tokenized gold products globally and is accessible via the HSBC HK app. Ownership details are recorded and maintained on a private permissioned DLT operated by HSBC. The product is also available to institutional clients in the UK.

initiative: BNY Tokenized Deposits Launch
description: BNY launched a tokenized deposit service enabling clients to transfer funds using blockchain rails, creating on-chain mirrored representations of client deposit balances on its Digital Assets platform. The service targets collateral and margin workflow use cases and operates on BNY's private permissioned blockchain. Initial participants included ICE, Citadel, and Ripple Prime, with ICE planning to integrate tokenized deposits across its clearinghouses for 24/7 trading.

initiative: BNY Digital Asset Data Insights – On-Chain Data for BlackRock BUIDL
description: BNY expanded its Digital Asset Platform by launching Digital Asset Data Insights, broadcasting select fund accounting data to the Ethereum network via smart contracts. BlackRock was the first client, with BNY broadcasting fund accounting data for the BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL). This enables on-chain participants to leverage distributed ledger technology for investment decisions.

initiative: BNY Digital Asset Custody for Bitcoin and Ethereum ETPs
description: BNY became the first and only U.S. Global Systemically Important Bank (G-SIB) to offer live digital asset custody for Bitcoin and Ethereum, focused on its Exchange-Traded Product (ETP) clients. The SEC did not oppose BNY's approach of excluding crypto assets from balance sheet liabilities, creating a precedent under SAB 122. BNY's Digital Asset Platform provides a single integrated infrastructure for safekeeping, servicing, and management of digital assets.

initiative: Deutsche Bank BaFin Crypto Custody License Application
description: Deutsche Bank applied for a digital asset custody license from Germany's financial regulator BaFin, marking a significant step in its expansion into cryptocurrency services. The move is part of a broader strategy to build a dedicated digital assets division. Deutsche Bank aims to launch its digital assets custody service in 2026 and has partnered with Bitpanda's technology unit to build the offering.

initiative: Deutsche Bank – KfW Tokenized Bond (Project Guardian)
description: Deutsche Bank served as bookrunner for KfW's first tokenized bond issued in July 2024 — a 100 million euro, 18-month bond and one of the largest DLT-based transactions in Germany at the time. Also in May 2024, Deutsche Bank joined the Monetary Authority of Singapore's Project Guardian asset and wealth management workstream to test feasibility of asset tokenization in regulated financial markets.

initiative: Deutsche Bank ECB DLT Exploratory Work – Tokenized Deposits & Blockchain Payments
description: Deutsche Bank participated in the ECB's DLT exploratory work, which saw 60 industry participants test blockchain-based settlement of central bank money. In November 2024, Deutsche Bank and UBS successfully completed a blockchain-based corporate payments experiment leveraging the Bundesbank's Trigger Solution and bank-issued tokenized deposits. The trials covered a range of securities and payments use cases, with €1.6 billion settled over a six-month period.

initiative: UBS Tokenize – Full-Service Digital Asset Platform
description: UBS launched UBS Tokenize, a full-service platform for digital asset services covering origination, distribution, and custody, initially focusing on tokenization of bonds, funds, and structured products. Assets are issued as smart contracts registered on public Ethereum or other qualifying DLT networks. UBS Tokenize also offers a tokenization service for FX and deposits following successful proof of concepts with clients.

initiative: UBS uMINT – First Live Tokenized Fund Transaction via Chainlink DTA
description: UBS and DigiFT completed the world's first in-production, end-to-end tokenized fund workflow leveraging the Chainlink Digital Transfer Agent (DTA) technical standard. The transaction involved a live subscription and redemption of the UBS USD Money Market Investment Fund Token (uMINT), built on Ethereum. The transaction proves that fund operations can be seamlessly automated on-chain for institutional-grade, compliant, and scalable workflows.

initiative: UBS and Deutsche Bank Tokenized Deposit Payments Experiment
description: UBS and Deutsche Bank successfully concluded a blockchain-based corporate payments experiment leveraging the Bundesbank's Trigger Solution and bank-issued tokenized deposits. This was part of the ECB's broader DLT exploratory work involving over 60 industry participants. The experiment demonstrated how tokenized deposits can enable real-time payment flows between major banks on DLT infrastructure.

initiative: Tokenized Deposit Solution in Hong Kong (HKD, CNH, USD) with Ant International
description: Standard Chartered Bank Hong Kong (SCBHK) launched a blockchain-based tokenized deposit solution under the HKMA's Project Ensemble, enabling 24/7 real-time transfers in HKD, CNH, and USD for Ant International's treasury management platform (Whale). The solution was co-created with Ant International and provides seamless near-real-time liquidity across entities. Both firms are members of EnsembleTX, which promotes DLT adoption in Hong Kong.

initiative: Tokenized Deposit Solution in Singapore (SGD, USD) with Ant International
description: Standard Chartered Singapore launched a tokenized SGD and USD account balances solution for Ant International on the MAS Guardian initiative, enabling real-time 24/7 movement of value. The solution complements the Hong Kong deployment and sets a blueprint for commercial banks to facilitate movement of funds from traditional ledgers to blockchain. Both Standard Chartered and Ant International are members of MAS's Guardian initiative.

initiative: Libeara – Tokenized Gold Fund (SC Ventures)
description: Libeara, a blockchain infrastructure platform supported by SC Ventures (Standard Chartered's venture arm), launched a tokenized gold fund (MG 999) in Singapore targeting institutional and accredited investors. The fund tracks gold spot prices via blockchain tokens on Libeara's ledger without vaulting costs. Standard Chartered also serves as custodian for the ULTRA tokenized US Treasury fund, supporting over $1 billion in compliant tokenized assets globally via Libeara.

initiative: Libeara & Mastercard – Tokenized Carbon Credits PoC (HKMA Project Ensemble)
description: Standard Chartered, via Libeara (incubated by SC Ventures), partnered with Mastercard and Mox Bank on a proof-of-concept pilot under the HKMA's Project Ensemble. The PoC demonstrated atomic swaps between tokenized carbon credits and tokenized deposits. This was an early demonstration of Standard Chartered's broader tokenization strategy in the real-world asset space.

initiative: SG-FORGE – First US Digital Bond on Canton Network
description: Societe Generale, via its digital assets subsidiary SG-FORGE, successfully completed its first digital bond issuance in the United States using Broadridge's tokenization capability on the Canton Network blockchain. The short-term, SOFR-linked floating rate bonds were the first securities tokenized using Broadridge's newly launched tokenization capability and were purchased by trading firm DRW. BNY acted as paying agent.

initiative: SG-FORGE EUR CoinVertible – Expansion to XRP Ledger
description: Societe Generale's digital asset subsidiary SG-FORGE launched its euro stablecoin EUR CoinVertible (EURCV) on the XRP Ledger, making it the third public blockchain to host EURCV after Ethereum (April 2023) and Solana (June 2025). EURCV is the first MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin designed for multi-chain deployment. SG-FORGE is also the issuer of the USD CoinVertible, launched in June 2025.

initiative: SG-FORGE EUR CoinVertible – MiCA Compliant Stablecoin on Ethereum
description: SG-FORGE issued EUR CoinVertible (EURCV), the first bank-issued euro stablecoin compliant with Europe's MiCA regulation, originally launched on Ethereum. The product is broadly available through SG-FORGE, exchanges, and market makers. EURCV can be subscribed 1:1 by sending EUR to SG-FORGE and redeemed 1:1 against fiat, and uses the CAST open-source framework for institutional compliance.

initiative: AssetFoundry – Tokenized Money Market Fund on Public Ethereum Blockchain
description: BNP Paribas Asset Management issued a tokenized share class of an existing French-domiciled money market fund on the public Ethereum network via BNP Paribas' AssetFoundry platform. The tokenized shares used a permissioned access model, restricting holdings and transfers to authorized participants while leveraging Ethereum's public infrastructure. BNP Paribas Securities Services acted as transfer agent, and this followed an earlier 2025 private blockchain experiment.

initiative: BNP Paribas Fnality Sterling Payment System – Interest Rate Swap Settlement
description: BNP Paribas completed its first interest payment swap with Lloyds Bank on the Sterling Fnality Payment System (£FnPS), a central bank-regulated DLT-based wholesale payment system. The transaction demonstrated how DLT can support real-time, risk-free settlement of complex financial instruments like interest rate swaps using digital representations of central bank money. BNP Paribas joined Fnality's network as part of its DLT infrastructure strategy.

initiative: BNP Paribas DLT-Based Digital Bond Tokenization Programme
description: BNP Paribas Global Markets developed its own tokenization platform over three years to test benefits, infrastructure changes, and process impacts of blockchain. The bank issued its first digital bond for its own Treasury department and has since opened initiatives to external issuers and investors. BNP Paribas partners across the Group to trial integrated digital assets spanning origination, distribution, and custody, including DvP settlement of digital bonds against digital cash.

initiative: Barclays Investment in Ubyx – Stablecoin Clearing Infrastructure
description: Barclays made its first equity investment linked to stablecoins by acquiring a stake in US-based Ubyx, a clearing system for digital money founded in 2025. Ubyx aims to create a many-to-many clearing network to unify fragmented stablecoin and tokenized deposit redemption. Barclays also joined a consortium of ten banks in October 2025 exploring a jointly issued stablecoin pegged to G7 currencies.

initiative: Barclays Blockchain Platform Exploration for Payments and Deposits
description: Barclays is sounding out technology providers on building a blockchain platform for payments and deposits infrastructure, including stablecoins and tokenized deposits. The bank issued a request for information to potential technology suppliers as it evaluates how to build out new digital offerings. This follows Barclays joining a consortium of major banks in October 2025 to explore a jointly issued G7-currency stablecoin.

initiative: Morgan Stanley Digital Trust National Association – OCC Charter Application
description: Morgan Stanley filed an application with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) for a national trust bank charter — Morgan Stanley Digital Trust, National Association — focused on digital asset custody and fiduciary services for wealth management clients. The charter would allow Morgan Stanley to custody client digital assets under federal regulation as part of a broader vertically integrated crypto strategy including ETFs, E*TRADE crypto trading, and custody.

initiative: Morgan Stanley E*TRADE Crypto Trading via Zerohash Partnership
description: Morgan Stanley announced a partnership with Zerohash in September 2025 to enable crypto trading through its E*TRADE platform, with launch planned for the first half of 2026. Morgan Stanley also invested in Zerohash as a strategic partner for its crypto infrastructure, building a vertically integrated crypto value chain spanning ETFs, retail trading, and institutional custody. In January 2026, Morgan Stanley filed ETF applications for spot Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana.

initiative: Morgan Stanley Digital Asset Strategy – Tokenization and Blockchain Infrastructure
description: Morgan Stanley's Head of Digital Asset Strategy Amy Oldenburg confirmed the bank's intention to develop in-house custody and exchange solutions for Bitcoin, including yield and lending services. The bank has been hiring for cryptocurrency and DeFi/tokenization infrastructure roles as part of its broader digital assets strategy. Morgan Stanley's Global Investment Committee expects crypto to deliver returns of around 6% over a seven-year horizon.

initiative: BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL)
description: Launched in March 2024 as BlackRock's first tokenized fund on a public blockchain, BUIDL invests 100% of its assets in cash, U.S. Treasury bills, and repurchase agreements, paying daily accrued dividends on-chain. Tokenized by Securitize on Ethereum, it expanded to six additional blockchains including Solana, Aptos, Avalanche, and Optimism, surpassing $2.5 billion in AUM by November 2025 and distributing approximately $100 million in dividends within its first year.

initiative: BUIDL Expansion to BNB Chain and Binance Collateral
description: In November 2025, BlackRock and Securitize launched a new BUIDL share class on BNB Chain, simultaneously enabling the fund to be accepted as off-exchange collateral for trading on Binance. Cross-chain interoperability is powered by Wormhole, with 68% of BUIDL's AUM now deployed beyond Ethereum as of that date.

initiative: iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHB)
description: BlackRock launched its iShares Staked Ethereum Trust (Nasdaq: ETHB) on March 12, 2026, debuting with approximately $100 million in assets on Nasdaq. The fund stakes 70–95% of its ETH holdings to generate rewards, distributing 82% of staking returns to investors monthly, with Coinbase serving as custodian and staking provider.

initiative: 2026 Thematic Outlook: Crypto and Tokenization as Investment Themes
description: BlackRock's 2026 Thematic Outlook formally identified cryptocurrency and tokenized assets alongside AI and geopolitics as key market drivers, with CEO Larry Fink describing tokenization as 'the next generation of financial markets.' The report highlighted Ethereum's role hosting approximately $12.5 billion in tokenized real-world assets (65% of the distributed market) and BlackRock's broader digital asset strategy encompassing Bitcoin ETF, Ethereum ETF, BUIDL, and stablecoin infrastructure.

initiative: Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund (FOBXX / BENJI)
description: Launched in April 2021 as the world's first U.S.-registered mutual fund to use a public blockchain as the system of record, FOBXX (tokenized as BENJI) uses Franklin Templeton's proprietary Benji Technology Platform. The fund expanded to the Avalanche network in August 2025 and enables USDC stablecoin subscriptions, peer-to-peer transfers on-chain, and 24/7/365 transfers. As of early 2026, the fund held approximately $864 million in net assets.

initiative: Franklin OnChain U.S. Dollar Short-Term Money Market Fund (Singapore)
description: Franklin Templeton launched a tokenized money market fund structured under Singapore's Variable Capital Company (VCC) framework in December 2025 via the InvestaX platform, becoming the first tokenized fund registered for retail investors with the Monetary Authority of Singapore. The fund invests in USD-denominated short-term debt and money market instruments with share ownership recorded on the Stellar blockchain via a blockchain-integrated transfer agent system.

initiative: First Fully Tokenized UCITS Fund in Luxembourg
description: In 2024, Franklin Templeton used the Benji Technology Platform to launch the first fully tokenized UCITS fund in Luxembourg, representing a major expansion of blockchain-based fund infrastructure into European regulated investment structures. The platform supports wallet-to-wallet transfers on public blockchains and stablecoin-based subscriptions and redemptions.

initiative: Fidelity Treasury Digital Fund OnChain Class (FYOXX)
description: Fidelity quietly launched the OnChain share class of its Fidelity Treasury Digital Fund (ticker: FYOXX) in August 2025, making it Fidelity's first on-chain tokenized product. The fund, recorded on Ethereum with BNY Mellon as custodian, invests at least 99.5% in U.S. Treasuries and reached approximately $200 million in assets. The Fidelity Digital Interest Token (FDIT) was subsequently launched in September 2025 as its on-chain representation, with Ondo Finance's OUSG as the initial anchor investor holding over 99% of assets.

initiative: Fidelity Digital Dollar (FIDD) Stablecoin
description: Launched on February 4, 2026, the Fidelity Digital Dollar (FIDD) is Fidelity's first stablecoin, issued by Fidelity Digital Assets, National Association (a nationally chartered trust bank), operating on the Ethereum blockchain. FIDD is redeemable 1:1 for USD, backed by cash and short-term U.S. Treasuries managed by Fidelity Management & Research Company, and is available on Fidelity Digital Assets, Fidelity Crypto, and major crypto exchanges.

initiative: WisdomTree Treasury Money Market Digital Fund (WTGXX) with 24/7 Trading
description: On February 24, 2026, WisdomTree launched 24/7 trading and instant settlement for its tokenized Treasury Money Market Digital Fund (WTGXX), the first time registered tokenized mutual fund shares have been permitted to trade and instantly settle around the clock within the U.S. regulatory perimeter. This required coordinated SEC exemptive relief and FINRA approval, enabling dealer-principal liquidity with USDC as the settlement stablecoin — a structural innovation compared since the introduction of ETFs.

initiative: WisdomTree Expands Full Tokenized Fund Suite to Solana
description: In January 2026, WisdomTree expanded its full suite of regulated tokenized funds to the Solana blockchain via the WisdomTree Connect and WisdomTree Prime platforms, enabling both institutional and retail investors to mint, trade, and hold tokenized funds natively on Solana. Key capabilities include native minting, stablecoin (USDC, PYUSD) subscriptions, and self-custody with portability to Solana-native DeFi applications.

initiative: WisdomTree Private Credit and Alternative Income Digital Fund (CRDT)
description: Launched in September 2025, CRDT is a tokenized private credit fund available to both retail and institutional investors via WisdomTree Prime and WisdomTree Connect. Tokenized on Ethereum and Stellar blockchains, the fund offers T+0 settlement for subscriptions, daily liquidity, and a minimum investment of $25, making traditionally institutional-grade private credit accessible on-chain.

initiative: WisdomTree Equity Premium Income Digital Fund (EPXC)
description: In December 2025, WisdomTree launched EPXC (ticker: WTPIX), a tokenized options overlay equity fund tracking the Volos U.S. Large Cap Target 2.5% PutWrite Index — the fifteenth tokenized fund on its platforms. The fund generates income from selling put options bi-weekly and is available to both retail (WisdomTree Prime) and institutional (WisdomTree Connect) investors.

initiative: Hamilton Lane SCOPE Fund Tokenization via KAIO on Sei Network
description: In October 2025, Hamilton Lane made its Senior Credit Opportunities Fund (SCOPE) accessible on-chain via a feeder structure (HL SCOPE Access Fund) on the Sei Network through KAIO's institutional-grade infrastructure. SCOPE is an all-weather senior private credit evergreen vehicle offering monthly liquidity and immediate capital deployment, representing Hamilton Lane's continued expansion of its tokenized private markets offerings.

initiative: Allfunds Blockchain Tokenization Partnership for European Access
description: In December 2024, Hamilton Lane partnered with Allfunds Blockchain and Apex Group (as transfer agent) to offer tokenized versions of select Hamilton Lane funds via the Allfunds Blockchain platform, targeting European investors across Spain, Italy, and other regions. The initiative aims to leverage fractionalization, improved efficiency, and streamlined administration to broaden private markets access.

initiative: Strategic Investment in Republic On-Chain Investment Platform
description: Announced in March 2026, Hamilton Lane made a strategic investment in Republic, a leading on-chain global investment platform, reinforcing their shared commitment to expanding retail investor access to institutional private market funds. The partnership builds on a prior collaboration providing retail access to Hamilton Lane's Private Infrastructure Fund (HLPIF) via Republic's platform.

initiative: Securitize Funding Round Strategic Investment
description: In May 2024, Hamilton Lane participated as a strategic investor in Securitize's $47 million funding round led by BlackRock, signaling its commitment to the broader tokenization infrastructure ecosystem. Hamilton Lane's investment reinforces its multi-year partnership with Securitize, which has tokenized numerous Hamilton Lane fund products.

initiative: KKR Health Care Strategic Growth Fund II Tokenization (SKHC) via Securitize
description: In September 2022, Securitize launched a tokenized feeder fund providing exposure to KKR's Health Care Strategic Growth Fund II on the Avalanche public blockchain — marking the first time a major global investment manager tokenized a fund on-chain in the U.S. Available to accredited investors under SEC Reg D 506(c), the initiative lowered investment minimums and improved digital onboarding and compliance protocols.

initiative: Apollo Diversified Credit Securitize Fund (ACRED)
description: In January 2025, Apollo and Securitize launched ACRED, a tokenized feeder fund providing on-chain access to Apollo's Diversified Credit Fund — a diversified global credit strategy spanning corporate direct lending, asset-backed lending, and structured credit. Initially available on six blockchains (Aptos, Avalanche, Ethereum, Ink, Polygon, Solana), ACRED grew to $112 million in AUM by September 2025 and expanded to Sei blockchain that month.

initiative: Apollo and Coinbase Asset Management Tokenized Credit Partnership
description: In October 2025, Coinbase Asset Management and Apollo announced a partnership to bring Coinbase stablecoin credit strategies to market, with planned product launches in 2026. Strategies include over-collateralized asset lending, corporate direct lending, and tokenized credit holdings, combining Apollo's private credit expertise with Coinbase's blockchain infrastructure.

initiative: State Street Digital Asset Platform Launch
description: On January 15, 2026, State Street launched its Digital Asset Platform — a secure, scalable infrastructure for tokenized assets built on a client partnership model. The platform includes wallet management, custodial, and cash capabilities, supports tokenized MMFs, ETFs, tokenized assets, and digital cash products (tokenized deposits and stablecoins) across both private and public permissioned blockchains. State Street selected Taurus as its custody and tokenization technology collaborator.

initiative: State Street Galaxy Onchain Liquidity Sweep Fund (SWEEP)
description: Announced in December 2025, State Street Investment Management and Galaxy Asset Management partnered to launch SWEEP, a tokenized private liquidity fund for qualified purchasers with planned $200 million seed investment from Ondo Finance. SWEEP enables on-chain cash management using PYUSD stablecoins, targeting a Q1 2026 launch on Solana with subsequent integrations on Stellar and Ethereum using Chainlink for cross-chain functionality.

initiative: Tokenized Money Market Fund via Archax on Hedera
description: State Street's money market fund was added to Archax's tokenized fund platform on the Hedera blockchain in November 2024, alongside Fidelity International, BlackRock, and Legal & General. Archax subsequently created a Pool Token on Hedera incorporating equal parts of money market funds from Aberdeen, BlackRock, State Street, and LGIM — effectively a natively digital fund-of-funds for MMFs.

initiative: Abrdn LUX Sterling Money Market Fund Tokenization via Archax on Hedera
description: Abrdn (formerly Aberdeen) launched its first blockchain-based investment by tokenizing interests and payouts of its £15 billion LUX Sterling money market fund on Hedera using Archax's Tokenization Engine — among the earliest major UK asset manager tokenization initiatives. The structure enables near-instantaneous asset transfers, reduced settlement times, and on-chain distribution of dividends as tokens, and was operational by early 2024.

initiative: Aberdeen Investments UK-First Tokenized Collateral FX Trade with Lloyds
description: In July 2025, Abrdn (Aberdeen Investments), Lloyds Banking Group, and Archax completed a UK-first transaction using tokenized units of Aberdeen's money market fund and tokenized UK gilts as collateral for foreign exchange trades between Aberdeen and Lloyds. The digital tokens were issued, transferred, and held by Archax on the Hedera blockchain, demonstrating regulated digital assets as viable collateral under existing UK legal frameworks.

initiative: Public Blockchain Strategy and Hedera Governing Council Membership
description: In early 2024, Abrdn articulated its public blockchain strategy, joining the governing council of Hedera Hashgraph and advocating for public (vs. permissioned) blockchain tokenization as the path to scale. The firm highlighted benefits of public chains for secondary market trading, liquidity, self-custody, and cross-platform interoperability for tokenized money market funds.

initiative: Active Crypto ETF SEC Filing (S-1)
description: T. Rowe Price filed an S-1 registration statement with the SEC in October 2025 and amended it in March 2026 for a proposed actively managed Crypto ETF investing directly in digital assets. The amended filing lists 15 eligible digital assets (including BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, AVAX, SHIB, and SUI), names Anchorage Digital Bank as crypto custodian, and expands share creation and redemption disclosures — representing a significant strategic shift for the $1.8 trillion traditional asset manager.

initiative: Digital Assets Return Framework Research (2025)
description: In January 2025, T. Rowe Price published a comprehensive white paper 'The Cryptocurrency Moment: Return Considerations for a New Asset Class,' formally affirming that digital assets have economic merit with value 'greater than zero.' The firm described itself as moving from a 'Niche Use Cases' scenario to 'Esoteric Allocation' and toward 'Accepted Asset Class,' signaling a material shift in institutional posture toward digital asset integration.

initiative: Invesco Private Credit Fund Tokenization via DigiFT (Singapore)
description: In February 2025, Invesco partnered with DigiFT — a Singapore MAS-regulated on-chain exchange — to tokenize a $6.3 billion senior private credit fund, marking Invesco's first steps into real-world asset tokenization. Institutional and accredited investors can access the fund using USD, USDC, or USDT, with the first-of-its-kind tokenized senior secured loan strategy offering daily liquidity on DigiFT's platform.

initiative: Digital Assets Strategy and Tokenization Panel (Breakthrough 2025)
description: At Invesco's flagship Breakthrough 2025 conference in December 2025, Kathleen Wrynn, Global Head of Digital Assets at Invesco, led a panel titled 'Building the Next Generation of Financial Products with Digital Assets,' featuring Galaxy, Superstate, and Solana Foundation. The event highlighted tokenization as central to Invesco's next-generation product strategy, with crypto ETPs and on-chain financial products as key focus areas.

initiative: USDC Stablecoin Settlement – U.S. Launch
description: Visa launched USDC settlement in the United States, enabling U.S. issuer and acquirer partners to settle VisaNet obligations in Circle's USDC stablecoin over the Solana blockchain. Initial participants include Cross River Bank and Lead Bank. Visa reported a $3.5 billion annualized stablecoin settlement run rate as of November 2025, with broader rollout planned through 2026.

initiative: Visa Tokenized Asset Platform (VTAP)
description: Visa launched VTAP in October 2024, a platform enabling banks to mint, burn, and manage fiat-backed tokens (stablecoins and tokenized deposits) on blockchain networks including public Ethereum. Spanish bank BBVA was the first major partner, testing VTAP in sandbox and targeting a live production pilot in 2025. The platform is accessible via APIs on the Visa Developer Platform.

initiative: Visa / Bridge (Stripe) Stablecoin Card Expansion
description: Visa and Stripe-owned Bridge announced expansion of their stablecoin-backed card issuance program from 18 countries (launched in 2025) to over 100 jurisdictions by end of 2026, covering Europe, Africa, Asia-Pacific, and the Middle East. The cards allow users to make everyday purchases using tokens in crypto wallets like Phantom and MetaMask at over 175 million Visa merchant locations. Visa is also piloting stablecoin settlement integration for card issuers and acquirers through the partnership.

initiative: Stablecoin-Linked Crypto Card Programs
description: Spending through Visa-issued crypto cards surged 525% in 2025, with total net transaction volume reaching $146 million by year-end. Visa supports over 130 stablecoin-linked card programs in more than 40 countries and facilitates stablecoin transactions across four blockchains. In December 2025, Visa established a dedicated stablecoin advisory team to help banks, merchants, and fintechs launch and manage stablecoin-related products.

initiative: Multi-Token Network (MTN) Expansion
description: Mastercard expanded its Multi-Token Network (MTN), a private permissioned blockchain serving as a settlement layer for tokenized bank deposits and regulated stablecoins including USDC, PYUSD, USDG, and SoFiUSD. The network supports 24/7 real-time interbank settlement and has integrated institutions including JPMorgan Chase, Standard Chartered, Ondo Finance (tokenized Treasuries), and Fiserv. MTN enables cross-border B2B payments, merchant stablecoin settlement, and programmable treasury management.

initiative: Crypto Partner Program (85+ companies)
description: Mastercard launched a global Crypto Partner Program bringing together more than 85 digital asset companies, traditional financial institutions, and payments providers to build infrastructure for cross-border transfers, B2B payments, and global payouts. The program is built around Mastercard's MTN and includes Circle (USDC), JPMorgan Chase, Standard Chartered, and dozens of crypto-native firms. Annual stablecoin-linked card spending reached $4.5 billion in 2025, a 673% year-over-year increase.

initiative: BVNK Acquisition ($1.8 Billion)
description: Mastercard announced a definitive agreement to acquire BVNK, a London-based stablecoin infrastructure company, for up to $1.8 billion (including $300 million in contingent payments). BVNK will power stablecoin capabilities across Mastercard's payment endpoints, enable 24/7 stablecoin settlement for processors and acquirers, and add stablecoin checkout to Mastercard's payment gateway. The deal is the largest stablecoin acquisition in industry history, eclipsing Stripe's $1.1 billion purchase of Bridge.

initiative: SoFiUSD Settlement Partnership
description: Mastercard partnered with SoFi to make SoFiUSD — the first stablecoin issued by a U.S. nationally chartered and insured deposit bank — available as a settlement option across Mastercard's global payments network. The collaboration aims to enable issuers and acquirers to settle card-based transactions with Mastercard using SoFiUSD, targeting cross-border remittances, B2B transfers, and digital asset payments. This marked the inaugural use of a stablecoin from a federally chartered U.S. bank in a major card network settlement capacity.

initiative: PYUSD Global Expansion to 70 Markets
description: PayPal expanded its PYUSD dollar-backed stablecoin to users across 70 markets globally, including countries in Asia-Pacific, Europe, Latin America (Colombia, Peru, Costa Rica, Panama), Africa, and North America. Users can now buy, hold, send, receive, and earn 4% annual rewards on PYUSD inside their PayPal accounts, with the ability to transfer to third-party digital wallets. PayPal's PYUSD market cap grew more than fivefold to $4.1 billion in the twelve months preceding the expansion.

initiative: Pay with Crypto for Merchants
description: PayPal launched 'Pay with Crypto, powered by PayPal,' a merchant-facing feature enabling sellers to accept digital currency (including PYUSD) with near-instant settlement, transaction rates of 0.99% (up to 90% lower than international credit card processing), and automatic conversion to fiat or PYUSD. Merchants can also earn 4% APY on PYUSD holdings. The product provides merchants access to a $3+ trillion crypto market while shielding them from volatile asset exposure.

initiative: PYUSD Expansion to TRON via Stargate Hydra
description: PayPal USD (PYUSD) expanded onto the TRON network through Stargate Hydra as PYUSD0, using LayerZero's Omnichain Fungible Token (OFT) standard to enable cross-chain asset movement. The PYUSD0 token is interchangeable with PYUSD and gives users in markets that rely heavily on TRON greater accessibility to the stablecoin. The expansion reinforces TRON's position as a key settlement layer in digital finance.

initiative: Bridge Acquisition ($1.1 Billion)
description: Stripe completed its $1.1 billion acquisition of stablecoin infrastructure platform Bridge in February 2025, its largest acquisition to date. Bridge enables companies to move, store, and accept stablecoins via a simple API, abstracting regulatory and compliance complexity, and was already used by SpaceX's Starlink and major platforms for cross-border payments. In its first week of offering stablecoin acceptance after re-enabling crypto, Stripe recorded more stablecoin volume than in its entire prior bitcoin transaction history.

initiative: Tempo Blockchain (Layer 1 for Payments)
description: Stripe, in partnership with crypto venture firm Paradigm, announced and launched Tempo on public testnet in late 2025 — a payments-first Layer 1 blockchain built around stablecoins with sub-second transaction finality, predictable dollar-denominated fees, and dedicated payment lanes for stablecoin transfers. Tempo raised $500 million in seed funding and counts Anthropic, OpenAI, Deutsche Bank, Shopify, Nubank, Klarna, Visa, and Revolut among its testnet partners. The mainnet launch is expected in 2026.

initiative: Bridge OCC Trust Bank Charter Approval
description: Stripe's Bridge platform received conditional approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) to operate as a national trust bank, paving the way for deeper institutional integration of stablecoin infrastructure. The approval positions Bridge as regulated stablecoin issuance and payment infrastructure, with Meta reportedly exploring Bridge as a partner for stablecoin-based creator payouts across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp in H2 2026.

initiative: Square Bitcoin Payments via Lightning Network
description: Block announced and launched 'Square Bitcoin' in October 2025, the first fully integrated bitcoin payments and wallet solution for merchants, using the Lightning Network for near-instant, low-fee transactions with zero processing fees in the first year. Merchants can accept bitcoin via QR code, manage holdings in a native bitcoin wallet alongside other business finances, and automatically convert a portion of daily card sales into bitcoin (Bitcoin Conversions). The rollout began with U.S. eligible sellers and is expected to reach all eligible Square sellers in 2026.

initiative: RLUSD Stablecoin Launch
description: Ripple launched RLUSD, its U.S. dollar-backed stablecoin, on December 17, 2024, following NYDFS approval, making it available on the Ethereum blockchain and the XRP Ledger on global exchanges including Bitso, Uphold, CoinMENA, MoonPay, and Archax. RLUSD is an enterprise-grade stablecoin designed for cross-border payments, institutional settlements, and DeFi collateral, and reached over $1 billion in circulation by December 2025. Ripple subsequently integrated RLUSD into its Ripple Payments solution in April 2025, used by cross-border payment providers BKK Forex and iSend.

initiative: Mastercard / WebBank / Gemini RLUSD Credit Card Settlement Pilot
description: At Ripple Swell 2025, Ripple announced a pilot with Mastercard, WebBank, and Gemini to settle credit card transactions using RLUSD on the XRP Ledger. In the pilot, WebBank (issuer of the Gemini Credit Card) uses RLUSD to settle payments on Mastercard's network via XRPL, compressing interbank settlement times from one to three days to seconds. This is one of the first instances of a regulated U.S. bank using a compliant stablecoin on a public ledger for traditional card payments.

initiative: USDC Growth & Circle Payments Network (CPN) Launch
description: Circle launched the Circle Payments Network (CPN) in May 2025, a global network for stablecoin-powered money movement reaching $3.4 billion in annualized transaction volume by year-end, with new corridors opened in Brazil, Nigeria, and other markets. USDC onchain volume reached $9.6 trillion in Q3 2025 (up 680% year-over-year), and USDC is now natively available on 30 blockchains. Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP) processed $31 billion in Q3 2025 alone (up 740% year-on-year) and $126 billion cumulatively as of December 2025.

initiative: Arc Layer-1 Blockchain Launch
description: Circle announced and launched the public testnet for Arc on October 28, 2025 — an open Layer-1 blockchain purpose-built for stablecoin finance, with USDC as native gas, a built-in FX engine for institutional-grade RFQ and 24/7 PvP settlement, and EVM compatibility. In its first 90 days, Arc testnet processed over 150 million transactions with ~1.5 million active wallets and average 0.5-second settlement. Visa is a design partner for Arc and plans to use it for USDC settlement within its network, also committing to operate a validator node.

initiative: t-0 Network Investment for Institutional Cross-Border Payments
description: Tether announced a strategic investment in the t-0 Network, a blockchain-based payments platform using USDT for instant cross-border settlements between licensed financial institutions. The t-0 platform enables real-time fiat-to-fiat settlement via USDT, using a net settlement model that reduces capital requirements and FX risk, and supports over 1,200 payment corridors. The move represents Tether's strategy to embed USDT into institutional financial infrastructure beyond crypto trading, backed by approximately $10 billion in 2025 profits from U.S. Treasury holdings.

initiative: Blockchain-Based Shared Ledger Announcement
description: Swift announced at Sibos 2025 (September 29, 2025) it would add a blockchain-based shared ledger to its technology infrastructure, developed in collaboration with over 30 global financial institutions including JPMorgan Chase, HSBC, Deutsche Bank, and DBS Bank. The ledger will use smart contracts for automated compliance, support real-time 24/7 cross-border payments, and interoperate with both existing fiat rails and emerging digital networks including stablecoins, tokenized deposits, and CBDCs. A prototype is being developed with Consensys.

initiative: Digital Asset Interoperability Trial (Tokenized Bonds)
description: Swift, together with BNP Paribas Securities Services, Intesa Sanpaolo, and Société Générale–FORGE, completed a landmark digital asset interoperability trial enabling the seamless exchange and settlement of tokenized bonds, with support for both fiat and digital currency payments. This was the first time Swift demonstrated its ability to orchestrate tokenized asset transactions as a single coordinated process across blockchain platforms and traditional systems. Prior trials also included work with Citi (fiat-digital settlement), HSBC and Ant International (ISO 20022 blockchain interoperability), and UBS Asset Management/Chainlink (bridging tokenized assets with existing payment systems).

initiative: RTP Network Record Growth & $10M Transaction Limit
description: The Clearing House's RTP (Real-Time Payments) network set records in 2024, processing $246 billion in payment value (up 94% YoY) and 343 million transactions (up 38% YoY). In February 2025, the per-transaction limit increased to $10 million, supporting growth in real estate, B2B, and cash concentration use cases. The RTP network, which uses ISO 20022 messaging standards, now averages over 1 million payments per day with 1,135 banks and credit unions enrolled as of January 2026, and processed more than $1.3 trillion in total payments in 2025.

institution: The Clearing House / Early Warning Services (Zelle)
initiative: Bank Stablecoin Consortium Exploration
description: Early Warning Services (operator of Zelle, owned by JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Capital One, PNC, Truist, and U.S. Bank) and The Clearing House announced in May 2025 that they are exploring a joint stablecoin initiative. In October 2025, Early Warning announced 'Zelle Goes International,' a formal initiative to expand Zelle globally by leveraging stablecoins for cross-border money movement. Wells Fargo has filed a trademark for 'WFUSD' and Bank of America has publicly stated readiness to launch a stablecoin once Congress provides final regulatory clarity.

initiative: FedNow Rapid Adoption and Scale
description: The Federal Reserve's FedNow instant payments service surpassed 1,600 participating financial institutions as of January 2026, with 2025 transaction volume increasing 460% over 2024. FedNow processed $853.4 billion in 2025 transaction value (up from $38.2 billion in 2024) and averages nearly 30,000 transactions per day. The per-transaction cap was raised to $10 million in November 2025, and at least one FI onboarded in as little as five days, compared to the prior fastest of seven days.

initiative: JPM Coin (JPMD) on Public Blockchain (Base/Ethereum L2)
description: JPMorgan's Kinexys division launched JPM Coin (ticker: JPMD), a USD-denominated deposit token, on Base (the Coinbase-built Ethereum L2 blockchain) in November 2025, marking the first time a major bank issued a deposit token on a public, permissionless blockchain. Clients including B2C2, Coinbase, and Mastercard completed proof-of-concept test transactions. JPMD enables near-instant 24/7 institutional settlement, peer-to-peer transfers between EVM-compatible wallets, and integration with smart contracts — processing over $1 billion daily on its private permissioned blockchain infrastructure.

initiative: SoFiUSD Stablecoin Launch
description: SoFi launched SoFiUSD in December 2025, becoming the first U.S. nationally chartered and insured deposit bank to issue a fully reserved stablecoin on a public, permissionless blockchain. SoFiUSD is fully backed 1:1 by U.S. dollars, with infrastructure provided by BitGo Bank & Trust and designed for faster settlements, 24/7 liquidity, and cross-border payment use cases. SoFiUSD reached $1 billion in circulation by March 2026 and is integrated into Mastercard's global payments network for card settlement.

initiative: Ondo Finance Joins Mastercard MTN (Tokenized Treasuries)
description: Mastercard and Ondo Finance partnered to make Ondo's Short-Term U.S. Government Treasuries Fund (OUSG) the first tokenized real-world asset integrated into the Mastercard Multi-Token Network, enabling businesses to earn daily yield on tokenized Treasuries with 24/7 subscriptions and redemptions on-chain. The integration allows institutional clients to hold tokenized Treasury assets as a form of onchain liquidity and collateral, bridging traditional TradFi with digital asset infrastructure.

initiative: RLUSD Integration into Ripple Payments
description: Ripple integrated RLUSD into its flagship Ripple Payments cross-border solution in April 2025, enabling enterprise customers to use the stablecoin for faster, compliant international transactions. Early customers including cross-border payment providers BKK Forex and iSend use RLUSD in live payment flows for treasury operations. RLUSD had reached $250 million in market capitalization and $10 billion in trading volume within months of its December 2024 launch, with $1.3 billion in circulation by November 2025.

initiative: Blockchain-Based Commercial Paper Issuance on Solana
description: JPMorgan arranged the issuance of short-term commercial paper for Galaxy Holdings on the Solana blockchain in December 2025, acquired by Coinbase and Franklin Templeton, with proceeds disbursed in USDC. This was one of the first institutional uses of a public blockchain for the issuance and management of traditional debt securities. JPMorgan's Markets division stated plans to broaden the structure in H1 2026 to additional issuers and security types.

initiative: NYSE Tokenized Securities Platform
description: The New York Stock Exchange announced development of a platform for trading and on-chain settlement of tokenized securities, pending regulatory approvals. The platform will enable 24/7 operations, instant settlement, dollar-denominated orders, and stablecoin-based funding, combining NYSE's Pillar matching engine with blockchain-based post-trade systems that support multiple chains for settlement and custody. ICE is simultaneously working with BNY and Citi to support tokenized deposits across its clearinghouses to facilitate around-the-clock margin management.

initiative: Nasdaq Tokenized Securities Rule Filing (SR-NASDAQ-2025-072)
description: Nasdaq filed a proposed rule change with the SEC in September 2025 to enable trading of securities in tokenized form on its exchange. The proposal was published in the Federal Register on January 30, 2026 for public comment and remains pending. It would allow Nasdaq to trade tokenized versions of securities consistent with the SEC's 2026 Staff Statement on Tokenized Securities, which classifies tokenized equities under the same federal rules as regular shares.

initiative: Nasdaq Equity Token Design (with Kraken/xStocks partnership)
description: On March 9, 2026, Nasdaq announced its equity token design framework, which keeps public issuers at the center of tokenized equity markets by tying blockchain records directly to the issuer's official share registry. The initiative includes a partnership with Kraken's parent company Payward to build an 'equities transformation gateway' bridging Nasdaq's regulated environment with open blockchain networks where xStocks operate. The program is expected to launch in H1 2027 and also targets modernizing proxy voting and corporate actions via programmable on-chain mechanisms.

initiative: Nasdaq–Börse Stuttgart Seturion Settlement Integration
description: Nasdaq announced it is linking its European trading venues to Seturion, the tokenized securities settlement platform operated by Börse Stuttgart Group, to reduce post-trade fragmentation across European capital markets. Seturion uses DLT to settle tokenized securities across public and private networks, supporting settlement in central bank money or on-chain cash. The collaboration initially focuses on structured products and is designed to operate within MiFID II and the EU DLT Pilot Regime.

initiative: CME–Google Cloud Universal Ledger (GCUL) Tokenization Pilot
description: CME Group and Google Cloud announced a pilot for wholesale payments and asset tokenization using Google Cloud Universal Ledger (GCUL), a private permissioned distributed ledger designed for financial institutions. CME completed Phase 1 integration and testing in March 2025, entered Phase 2 testing with settlement banks in mid-2025, and plans to launch new tokenization services in 2026. The focus is on tokenizing cash and non-cash assets within CME's existing clearing ecosystem to enable 24/7 capital mobility.

initiative: CME 24/7 Crypto Derivatives Trading Launch (Q2 2026)
description: CME Group announced plans to launch 24-hour, seven-day-a-week trading for its cryptocurrency futures and options in Q2 2026, extending institutional-grade crypto derivatives access beyond traditional market hours. This builds on CME's record crypto volumes in 2025, when it facilitated nearly $3 trillion notional in crypto futures and options. The move supports CME's broader digital asset strategy alongside its tokenization work with Google Cloud.

initiative: Cboe Digital Exchange Futures Migration to Cboe Futures Exchange (CFE)
description: Cboe completed the migration of its Cboe Digital Exchange bitcoin (FBT) and ether (FET) futures onto the Cboe Futures Exchange (CFE) on June 9, 2025, consolidating all U.S. futures products on one exchange platform. The cash-settled crypto futures, originally launched on Cboe Digital Exchange in January 2024, are now available on a nearly 24x5 basis alongside Cboe FTSE Bitcoin Index Futures. This integration enhances efficiency and customer access to both traditional and digital asset derivatives.

initiative: Cboe Continuous Bitcoin and Ether Futures
description: Cboe launched continuous (no-expiry) futures contracts for bitcoin and ether on December 15, 2025, providing a continuous long-term market exposure structure favored by crypto-native institutional participants. The contracts are listed on the Cboe Futures Exchange and represent an expansion of Cboe's institutional-grade crypto derivatives offering beyond traditional quarterly expirations. These contracts complement Cboe's bitcoin and ether standard futures already trading on CFE.

initiative: LSEG Digital Markets Infrastructure (DMI) Platform for Private Funds
description: LSEG launched its Digital Markets Infrastructure (DMI) platform on September 15, 2025, completing its first live transaction with MembersCap as General Partner and Archax as nominee for a web-3 foundation investor. Built on Microsoft Azure, DMI delivers blockchain-powered infrastructure for the full asset lifecycle—from issuance and tokenization through to post-trade settlement and servicing—starting with private funds and designed to expand to additional asset classes. The platform is interoperable with both DLT and traditional finance infrastructure.

initiative: LSEG Digital Securities Depository
description: LSEG announced on February 12, 2026 plans to build an on-chain settlement service for institutional investors called the LSEG Digital Securities Depository, which will facilitate trading and settlement of tokenized bonds, equities, and private market assets across multiple blockchain networks while maintaining compatibility with existing settlement systems. The first deliverable is expected in 2026 pending regulatory approval. LSEG plans to establish a strategic partner group and position the depository as a bridge between digital and traditional markets across time zones.

initiative: D7 DLT – Tokenized Securities Issuance Platform
description: Clearstream, a Deutsche Börse Group subsidiary and international CSD, launched D7 DLT on November 4, 2025—a live, CSDR-compliant tokenized securities issuance and lifecycle management platform built using Google Cloud technology. The platform follows successful live use cases conducted during the 2024 ECB Trials, including commercial paper and intraday repo transactions, and is being initially rolled out through Clearstream Banking S.A. for Eurobond market participants. D7 DLT complements the existing centralized D7 Digital platform (which has processed €44 billion in issuances) and integrates with Deutsche Börse's 360X DLT trading venue.

initiative: Clearstream Crypto Custody Service Launch
description: Clearstream announced on March 11, 2025 that it would offer institutional investors settlement and custody services for digital assets (cryptocurrencies), with the service launching in April 2025. Crypto Finance, another Deutsche Börse Group entity, acts as the sub-custodian for these digital assets. This makes Clearstream one of the first major global CSDs to directly offer institutional crypto custody infrastructure.

initiative: 360X DLT Multilateral Trading Facility (DLT-MTF) – First DLT-MTF License
description: 360X, a fintech jointly founded by Deutsche Börse and Commerzbank, became the only firm licensed under the EU DLT Pilot Regime as a pure DLT Multilateral Trading Facility (DLT-MTF) without settlement, offering trading for both DLT and non-DLT MiFID II assets. In August 2025, 360X completed the first secondary market transaction of Siemens' €300m digital bond on a regulated venue, settled in central bank money via the Bundesbank Trigger Solution within minutes. The platform holds both a conventional MTF license and a DLT-MTF license, bridging traditional and tokenized capital markets.

initiative: HKEX Synapse Settlement Acceleration Platform
description: HKEX launched Synapse in October 2023 as an integrated settlement acceleration platform for Northbound Stock Connect, deploying DAML smart contracts to standardize and streamline post-trade workflows. Developed in collaboration with DTCC, Synapse provides a centralized platform generating a single source of truth for settlement instruction workflows and enriching trades with Standing Settlement Instructions from DTCC's CTM matching solution. The platform reduces settlement risks and operational friction for international investors accessing China's equities market.

initiative: HKEX ETP Digitalization via DLT and Smart Contracts
description: HKEX disclosed plans in November 2024 to digitalize and automate the physical subscription and redemption mechanism for exchange-traded products (ETPs) through an online platform, leveraging DLT and smart contracts to connect major ETP market participants including issuers, authorized participants, and custodians. The initiative targets a go-live by 2025 and aims to bring efficiency and automation to the primary market subscription and redemption process. It builds on Hong Kong's broader push to digitalize capital market infrastructure.

initiative: SGX Derivatives Institutional Crypto Perpetual Futures
description: SGX Derivatives launched institutional-grade crypto perpetual futures contracts on November 24, 2025, providing a continuous, no-expiry structure that is favored by crypto-native communities. These contracts break new ground for SGX by expanding its derivatives product suite into crypto markets with institutional-grade infrastructure. The launch positions Singapore as a key regulated hub for institutional crypto derivatives in Asia.

institution: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) / SGX ecosystem
initiative: MAS Project Guardian – Fixed Income Framework and Fund Tokenization
description: Project Guardian, led by MAS and including SGX among its participants, published the Guardian Fixed Income Framework (GFIF) in November 2024 to guide tokenization in debt capital markets, and ICMA was named lead of the fixed income workstream in June 2025. The initiative expanded to over 40 industry participants by 2025, covering tokenized funds, fixed income, and structured products. In November 2025, MAS also launched a live trial of tokenized MAS bills settled via CBDC for overnight interbank lending, integrating tokenization into money market infrastructure.

institution: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) / SGX ecosystem
initiative: MAS BLOOM Initiative – Tokenized Settlement Assets
description: MAS launched BLOOM (Borderless, Liquid, Open, Online, Multi-currency) on October 16, 2025, a new initiative to extend settlement capabilities using tokenized bank liabilities and well-regulated stablecoins across G10 and Asian currencies for both domestic and cross-border transactions. BLOOM builds on Project Orchid's digital Singapore dollar pilots and incorporates programmable compliance controls and agentic payment capabilities. Members include Ant International, StraitsX, Coinbase, and DBS Bank, targeting seamless and automated digital settlement.

initiative: SDX Integration In-House – Digital Exchange Merging with SIX Main Operations
description: SIX Group announced in October 2025 that it is retiring the SDX (SIX Digital Exchange) brand and absorbing its digital asset operations in-house, with trading capabilities rolling into SIX's main exchange business and digital settlement and custody services moving to SIX's post-trade division. SDX was the world's first fully regulated digital financial market infrastructure for issuing, settling, and custodying regulated digital securities, and had reached over CHF 1 billion in assets by May 2024. The move mainstreams DLT-based infrastructure into SIX's core business rather than treating it as a separate subsidiary.

initiative: SIX Digital Collateral Service (DCS)
description: SIX launched the Digital Collateral Service (DCS) on February 12, 2025, allowing financial institutions to post selected cryptocurrency assets alongside traditional collateral in a unified collateral management framework. This is the first such service from a major European financial market infrastructure operator, enabling clients to manage both traditional securities and crypto collateral in a single platform. It reflects SIX's strategy to integrate digital assets into institutional financial workflows.

initiative: SIX Investment in OpenBrick Tokenized Securities Platform
description: SIX (via its Spanish subsidiary BME) announced an investment in OpenBrick on November 27, 2024—a digital platform for the issuance, management, trading, and settlement of tokenized securities linked to real assets. OpenBrick is designed to serve institutional participants seeking regulated tokenized securities infrastructure in Spain and across Europe. The investment reflects SIX Group's strategy to build out a European tokenized securities ecosystem alongside its SDX operations.

initiative: CHESS Replacement Program – TCS BaNCS (Post-DLT Pivot)
description: After abandoning its original blockchain-based CHESS replacement (built with Digital Asset Holdings) in November 2022 and writing down AUD 250 million, ASX signed Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to deliver a new CHESS replacement using the TCS BaNCS Market Infrastructure product. Release 1 (clearing) is targeting a go-live in April 2026, with internal parallel testing completed in late 2025 and industry testing progressing. Release 2 (settlement and sub-register) targets implementation in 2029, with the ASX also consulting on a possible move to T+1 settlement.

initiative: DTCC Smart NAV Pilot – On-Chain Mutual Fund NAV Data
description: DTCC, along with 10 market participants and Chainlink, completed the Smart NAV pilot in 2024, demonstrating how trusted mutual fund NAV (price and rate) data from DTCC's Mutual Fund Profile Service can be disseminated on-chain across virtually any blockchain using Chainlink's CCIP as the interoperability layer. The pilot found that structured on-chain data can power tokenized fund use cases, brokerage portfolio applications, and bulk consumer smart contracts. DTCC identified potential to expand the capability across a wider range of use cases beyond fund pricing.

initiative: DTCC DTC Tokenization Service – SEC No-Action Letter
description: On December 11, 2025, the SEC issued a No-Action Letter to DTCC's subsidiary DTC, authorizing a three-year, controlled-production tokenization service for DTC-custodied assets on pre-approved blockchains. The service applies to highly liquid assets including the Russell 1000, major index ETFs, and U.S. Treasury bills, bonds, and notes, and is underpinned by DTCC's ComposerX suite. Full production rollout is anticipated in the second half of 2026, with Year 1 focused on reconciliation, Year 2 on interoperability pilots, and Year 3 on scaling.

initiative: DTCC–Digital Asset–Canton Network Partnership for U.S. Treasury Tokenization
description: On December 17, 2025, DTCC and Digital Asset announced a partnership to tokenize DTC-custodied U.S. Treasury securities on the Canton Network, using DTCC's ComposerX suite. A minimum viable product in a controlled environment is planned for H1 2026, with broader industry rollout including additional DTC- and Fed-eligible assets expected in H2 2026. DTCC became co-chair of the Canton Foundation's governance alongside Euroclear, and the initiative builds on earlier live 24/7 onchain repo transaction pilots completed in July 2025.

initiative: DTCC Project Ion – DLT-Based Equity Settlement (R3 Corda)
description: DTCC's Project Ion, developed in collaboration with R3 using Corda DLT software, became operational as an MVP in August 2022 and has been running as a parallel settlement record providing an alternative infrastructure for bilateral equity settlement transactions, recording over 160,000 transactions on peak days. While the existing DTC classic system remains the authoritative record, Project Ion demonstrates T+0 netted settlement capability and scalability for DLT-based equity settlement. The initiative laid groundwork for DTCC's broader tokenization strategy, including its December 2025 SEC-authorized service.

initiative: DTCC Project Whitney – Private Markets Tokenization Prototype
description: DTCC's Project Whitney is a multi-phase initiative to evaluate the practical and commercial viability of blockchain-based infrastructure supporting private market securities through issuance, distribution, and secondary transfer. The prototype mints tokens on the public Ethereum network while maintaining an independent off-chain digital record of security ownership with real-time compliance and suitability checks. The project was designed to address private markets fragmentation, paper-based processes, and lack of institutional-grade infrastructure for tokenized securities.

initiative: Digital Asset Securities Interoperability Framework (with BCG)
description: On March 4, 2026, DTCC, Euroclear, and Clearstream—together with Boston Consulting Group—released a white paper titled 'Building the Path Towards Digital Asset Securities Interoperability,' outlining a framework for addressing DLT network fragmentation and enabling interoperability at scale. The paper proposes five key foundations: assets and liabilities, ownership recognition, asset lifecycle and movement protocols, ledgers, and legal/regulatory compliance. This builds on their 2024 joint paper 'Building the Digital Asset Ecosystem,' which established Digital Asset Securities Control Principles (DASCPs).

initiative: Euroclear D-FMI (Digital Financial Market Infrastructure) DLT Platform
description: Euroclear's D-FMI (Digital Financial Market Infrastructure) is a distributed ledger-based platform for issuing digitally native notes (DNNs) that bridge digital and traditional financial market infrastructure. On March 9, 2026, Citi issued the first digitally native structured note on the D-FMI platform, marking the first such instrument in the wealth management industry and expanding D-FMI from digital bonds to structured products. D-FMI-issued securities have been recognized as High-Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) and, from March 30, 2026, the ECB confirmed that DLT-issued securities will be eligible as Eurosystem collateral.

initiative: Eurobond Dematerialization – Clearstream & Euroclear Bank Live Infrastructure
description: As of March 16, 2026, both Clearstream and Euroclear Bank—the two International Central Securities Depositories (ICSDs)—went live with dematerialized Eurobond infrastructure for the €15.3 trillion Eurobond market, ending paper-based issuance starting with English law issuances. This dematerialization is a foundational step preceding full tokenization; Clearstream's Jens Hachmeister indicated that a tokenized Eurobond on the D7 DLT platform is expected imminently. The jointly developed Issuance & Processing Taxonomy (IPT) published in September 2025 also laid groundwork for a forthcoming DLT extension.

initiative: SWIFT Blockchain-Based Shared Ledger
description: At Sibos 2025 in Frankfurt on September 29, 2025, SWIFT CEO Javier Pérez-Tasso announced that SWIFT will add a blockchain-based shared ledger to its technology infrastructure, extending its platform into digital environments to enable real-time, 24/7 cross-border payments and trusted movement of tokenized value. The ledger is being designed with over 30 global financial institutions from 16 countries, with Consensys building the first conceptual prototype; it uses smart contracts to record, sequence, and validate transactions while maintaining interoperability with existing SWIFT rails. The first use case is real-time 24/7 interbank cross-border payments.

initiative: SWIFT Digital Asset Interoperability Trial (Live Tokenized Bond Settlement)
description: SWIFT completed a landmark digital asset interoperability trial in January 2026 with BNP Paribas Securities Services, Intesa Sanpaolo, and Société Générale–FORGE, demonstrating seamless issuance, exchange, settlement, and servicing of tokenized bonds across both traditional financial systems and blockchain platforms using fiat and digital currencies including stablecoins. The trial used SG-Forge's EURCV stablecoin and proved DvP settlement for tokenized bonds over SWIFT infrastructure. This followed earlier trials with UBS/Chainlink, Citi, HSBC/Ant International, and Northern Trust/Reserve Bank of Australia.

initiative: SWIFT Live Trials of Digital Asset Transactions (2025 Rollout)
description: SWIFT announced in October 2024 that starting in 2025, global financial institutions across North America, Europe, and Asia would conduct live pilot transactions for the settlement of digital assets and currencies over the SWIFT network. The pilots leverage SWIFT's global infrastructure to interlink various digital and traditional currency platforms, providing a single system for banks to transact across borders. SWIFT has also been named as a participant in Project Agora, the BIS-led initiative exploring integration of tokenized commercial bank deposits and wholesale CBDCs.

institution: OCC (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency)
initiative: OCC Interpretive Letter 1186 – Banks May Hold Crypto Assets for Network Fees
description: The OCC issued Interpretive Letter 1186 on November 18, 2025, confirming that national banks may pay blockchain network fees ('gas fees') and hold crypto assets on balance sheet as principal for limited operational purposes, including testing permissible crypto-related platforms. This guidance extends bank authority into on-chain operations, enabling banks to directly participate in blockchain-based settlement and tokenized asset infrastructure. It reflects a broader shift in the OCC's regulatory posture toward enabling banks to engage with digital asset networks.

institution: OCC (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency)
initiative: OCC Interpretive Letter 1188 – Riskless Principal Crypto-Asset Transactions + Five Digital Asset Trust Bank Approvals
description: On December 9, 2025, the OCC issued Interpretive Letter 1188 confirming that national banks may engage in riskless principal crypto-asset transactions (acting as intermediary without holding crypto in inventory). On December 12, 2025, the OCC also issued conditional approvals for five national trust bank applications to offer digital-asset-related products and services, including custody and settlement for tokenized assets and potential stablecoin issuance under the GENIUS Act framework. These actions, together with Interpretive Letters 1179 and 1183 earlier in 2025, establish a comprehensive framework enabling banks to engage with digital asset infrastructure.

initiative: Joint SEC-CFTC Crypto Asset Interpretation / Token Taxonomy Framework
description: The SEC and CFTC jointly issued an interpretation clarifying how federal securities laws apply to crypto assets, providing a coherent token taxonomy that distinguishes digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, and digital securities. SEC Chair Paul Atkins stated that most crypto assets are not themselves securities, addressing over a decade of market uncertainty. The guidance also clarifies how a non-security crypto asset may become subject to, or cease to be subject to, an investment contract.

initiative: SEC Crypto Task Force – No-Action Letters and Guidance Campaign
description: Under Chair Paul Atkins, the SEC's Crypto Task Force launched a broad program of no-action letters, interpretative statements, and FAQs clarifying the interplay between U.S. securities laws and distributed ledger technology. Key guidance included positions that meme coins purchased for entertainment or cultural purposes typically do not involve the offer and sale of securities, and that spot crypto products could potentially trade on registered exchanges. The Task Force also solicited extensive written input from industry stakeholders on tokenization, custody, and security classification.

initiative: No-Action Letter – State Trust Companies as Crypto Custodians
description: The SEC Division of Investment Management issued a no-action letter stating it would not recommend enforcement action against registered investment advisers or regulated funds that maintain crypto assets with certain state-chartered trust companies, treating such institutions as 'banks' for custody purposes under the Investment Advisers Act and Investment Company Act. This provided regulatory clarity for the crypto custody industry and signaled the SEC's more accommodative approach under the new administration.

initiative: CFTC Crypto Sprint – Tokenized Collateral No-Action Relief
description: CFTC Acting Chairman Caroline Pham launched a 12-month 'Crypto Sprint' in August 2025 to fast-track digital asset regulatory actions. In December 2025, CFTC staff issued guidance allowing futures commission merchants (FCMs) and derivatives clearing organizations to accept tokenized collateral, including stablecoins, Bitcoin, and Ether. The CFTC simultaneously withdrew Staff Advisory 20-34, which had restricted digital asset collateral, replacing it with a framework for FCMs accepting non-security digital assets as customer margin.

initiative: Joint SEC-CFTC Interpretation on Crypto Asset Jurisdiction
description: The CFTC joined the SEC's March 2026 interpretation to provide guidance that CFTC staff will administer the Commodity Exchange Act consistent with the SEC's interpretation of securities law, and that certain non-security crypto assets could meet the definition of 'commodity' under the CEA. CFTC Chair Michael Selig noted this provides the clearest articulation of the regulatory boundary between the SEC and CFTC for digital assets. The guidance establishes that digital commodities fall under CFTC jurisdiction.

institution: OCC (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency)
initiative: Interpretive Letters 1184, 1186, 1188 – Expanded Crypto Permissions for National Banks
description: The OCC issued a series of interpretive letters in 2025 expanding permissible crypto activities for national banks: Interpretive Letter 1184 addressed cryptoasset custody services; Letter 1186 (November 2025) confirmed banks may pay blockchain 'gas fees' and hold crypto as principal for that purpose; Letter 1188 (December 2025) confirmed banks may engage in riskless principal crypto transactions acting as intermediary between counterparties without holding inventory. These letters substantially expanded the scope of crypto engagement available to nationally chartered banks.

institution: OCC (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency)
initiative: GENIUS Act Proposed Rulemaking – Payment Stablecoin Framework
description: On February 25, 2026, the OCC issued a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to implement the GENIUS Act for national banks and federal qualified payment stablecoin issuers, proposing a new 12 CFR Part 15 establishing licensing, reserve requirements, prudential standards, custody rules, capital, and reporting requirements for payment stablecoin issuers. The OCC also proposed that a custodian demonstrates 'control' of tokenized assets if no other party can transfer the asset on a distributed ledger without the custodian's consent.

initiative: Rescission of 2023 Anti-Crypto Policy Statement – New Innovation-Friendly Framework
