description: In December 2025, the Federal Reserve rescinded its 2023 policy statement that had created a strong presumption against approval of novel and unprecedented crypto-asset activities for state member banks, including holding crypto-assets as principal and issuing crypto tokens. The new 2025 policy statement incorporates a 'different activity, different risks, different regulation' principle and may authorize uninsured state member banks to engage in activities not permissible for national banks, provided they meet adequate financial safety standards.

initiative: Rescission of FIL-16-2022 – New Crypto Activities Guidance
description: In March 2025, the FDIC rescinded FIL-16-2022, which had required FDIC-supervised institutions to obtain prior FDIC approval before engaging in any crypto-related activity. The new guidance affirms that FDIC-supervised institutions may engage in permissible crypto-related activities—including acting as custodians, maintaining stablecoin reserves, issuing digital assets, market making, and participating in DLT-based settlement systems—without prior agency approval, subject to normal risk management expectations.

initiative: GENIUS Act Proposed Rulemaking – Payment Stablecoin Issuer Application Process
description: In December 2025, the FDIC issued the first proposed rule under the GENIUS Act establishing an application process for subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised institutions seeking to become permitted payment stablecoin issuers. The FDIC will serve as primary federal regulator and licensor for such subsidiaries. This marked the first formal regulatory implementation of the landmark July 2025 stablecoin legislation.

initiative: GENIUS Act AML/BSA Implementation – Payment Stablecoin Compliance
description: The GENIUS Act, enacted July 18, 2025, brought payment stablecoins under the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), with FinCEN responsible for implementing AML and sanctions compliance rules for stablecoin issuers. The Act mandates comprehensive customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, suspicious activity reporting, and OFAC screening. The U.S. Treasury Department issued a proposed rulemaking in September 2025 soliciting input on BSA/AML obligations, balance of state and federal oversight, and comparable foreign regulatory regimes under the GENIUS Act.

initiative: Executive Order – Strengthening American Leadership in Digital Financial Technology
description: President Trump signed an executive order on January 23, 2025, marking a sweeping reversal of the prior administration's approach to digital assets. The order revoked EO 14067, directed all agencies to review digital asset regulations within 60 days, protected blockchain activities such as self-custody and permissionless transactions, banned establishment of a U.S. CBDC, and created the President's Working Group on Digital Asset Markets to coordinate a pro-innovation regulatory roadmap across federal agencies.

initiative: Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and U.S. Digital Asset Stockpile Executive Order
description: On March 6, 2025, President Trump signed an executive order establishing a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (treating Bitcoin as a reserve asset analogous to gold) and a separate U.S. Digital Asset Stockpile for other forfeited digital assets. The order directed all federal agencies to transfer government-held Bitcoin to the reserve and provide a full accounting of digital asset holdings to Treasury within 30 days. The Secretaries of Treasury and Commerce were directed to develop budget-neutral strategies for acquiring additional digital assets.

initiative: GENIUS Act – First Federal Stablecoin Regulatory Framework
description: The Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins (GENIUS) Act was enacted on July 18, 2025, becoming the first major piece of U.S. digital asset legislation. It establishes a licensing and supervision regime for payment stablecoin issuers, defines reserve and redemption requirements, and limits issuance to authorized bank subsidiaries or OCC-licensed entities. The Act subjects stablecoin issuers to bank-like prudential oversight including safety and soundness requirements, AML compliance, and consumer protections.

initiative: MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation) – Full Implementation
description: The EU's landmark Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) reached full implementation on December 30, 2024, extending licensing, disclosure, authorization, and supervisory requirements to all crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) across the EU. Stablecoin rules (for asset-referenced tokens and e-money tokens) had come into force June 30, 2024. A transitional grandfathering period allows existing CASPs in certain member states to continue operating until July 1, 2026, after which full MiCA authorization is required EU-wide.

initiative: MiCA Technical Standards and Guidelines Package
description: ESMA published extensive technical standards and guidelines throughout 2024-2025 to implement MiCA, including: Final Reports on market abuse detection and prevention standards for crypto assets; Guidelines on qualification of crypto assets as financial instruments; Technical standards for order book and recordkeeping; and Q&As on the scope of 'trading platforms for crypto-assets.' ESMA's 2026 Work Programme prioritizes supervisory convergence on CASP authorization and ongoing supervision across EU member states.

initiative: MiCA Transition Advisory and ART/EMT Supervision
description: The EBA issued its No-Action Letter (EBA/Op/2025/08) in June 2025 giving crypto asset service providers a nine-month transition period to continue transacting electronic money tokens while awaiting payment services authorization. In February 2026, the EBA issued guidance to national authorities on post-transition enforcement. The EBA also commenced steady-state supervision of significant issuers of asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs) under MiCA, and is preparing a 2026 factsheet on tokenized settlement assets used by EU banks.

initiative: Digital Euro – Preparation Phase Completion and Next Phase Launch
description: On October 30, 2025, the ECB Governing Council moved the digital euro project into a new phase following successful completion of the preparation phase begun in November 2023. If EU co-legislators adopt the Digital Euro Regulation in the course of 2026, a pilot exercise could start mid-2027, with full readiness for potential first issuance during 2029. Key achievements include development of the draft digital euro scheme rulebook, selection of technology providers, and an innovation platform for market participant experimentation.

initiative: Eurosystem DLT Settlement Strategy – Pontes and Appia Initiatives
description: The ECB announced a strategy to settle DLT-based transactions in central bank money through two complementary initiatives: 'Pontes,' an initial cash settlement solution targeting Q3 2026 launch, and 'Appia,' a longer-term vision for Europe's future digital finance ecosystem. This strategy aims to provide risk-free settlement assets that support the growth of an integrated European digital finance ecosystem. The ECB also supports the European Commission's proposal to reinforce the right to pay with cash alongside digital euro development.

initiative: Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 – New Crypto Regulatory Regime
description: On February 4, 2026, Parliament made the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026, bringing cryptoassets within the FCA's full regulatory remit. The new regime, set to come into force on October 25, 2027, creates regulated activities for dealing, arranging, advising, safeguarding, and operating trading platforms for qualifying cryptoassets and stablecoins. A market abuse regime for cryptoassets is also established. The FCA's authorization gateway opens September 30, 2026.

initiative: Consultation Papers CP25/40, CP25/41, CP25/42 – Crypto Prudential and Market Abuse Rules
description: In December 2025, the FCA published three major consultation papers to advance cryptoasset regulation: CP25/40 on rules and guidance for firms carrying out cryptoasset activities (including custody, trading, and staking); CP25/41 on admissions, disclosures, and a crypto-specific market abuse regime; and CP25/42 on a prudential regime for cryptoasset firms. The consultations also proposed applying the Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SM&CR) to cryptoasset firms, making senior managers personally accountable for operational failures.

initiative: Systemic Stablecoin Regulatory Regime and DLT Synchronisation Lab
description: The Bank of England proposed a regulatory regime for sterling-denominated systemic stablecoins focused on real-world payments and settlements, with the Bank serving as prudential regulator for stablecoins reaching systemic importance. In 2026, the Bank plans to launch a 'synchronisation lab' to enable testing of real-world use cases for DLT and digital assets, allowing the Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) system to interoperate with external ledgers including DLT-based systems. A joint FCA/BoE regulatory framework is expected in 2026.

initiative: Digital Token Service Providers Licensing Regime Under FSMA
description: On June 6, 2025, MAS announced that a new licensing regime for digital token service providers (DTSPs) under the Financial Services and Markets Act would take effect June 30, 2025. The regime requires Singapore-incorporated digital token service providers serving only overseas clients to obtain a Part 9 licence or cease operations, with licences granted only in limited circumstances due to heightened cross-border money laundering risks. No transitional period was provided, reflecting Singapore's firm stance on AML/CFT for crypto businesses with cross-border exposure.

initiative: Project Guardian – Tokenized Funds Operational Guide and Live CBDC Trials
description: In November 2025, MAS published an Operational Guide for Tokenized Funds as part of Project Guardian, providing fund managers, custodians, and service providers a shared framework for governance, NAV calculation, investor onboarding, and compliance. MAS also announced a live trial of tokenized MAS bills settled using a wholesale CBDC for overnight interbank lending, integrating tokenization into money market infrastructure. Project Guardian continues testing tokenization of structured products, investment vehicles, asset-backed securities, bonds, and bank liabilities.

initiative: Stablecoins Ordinance – Stablecoin Issuer Licensing Regime
description: Hong Kong's Stablecoins Ordinance came into effect on August 1, 2025, making issuance of fiat-referenced stablecoins a regulated activity requiring HKMA licensing. The HKMA published supervision guidelines, AML/CFT guidelines, and explanatory notes for licensing and transitional provisions for pre-existing issuers. As of September 2025, 36 companies had submitted applications, including notable applicants such as Standard Chartered/Animoca Brands/HKT consortium. Hong Kong planned to issue its first batch of stablecoin licenses in Q1 2026.

initiative: Project Ensemble TX – Tokenized Deposits Pilot (Real-Value Transactions)
description: On November 13, 2025, HKMA launched Ensemble TX, the pilot phase of Project Ensemble, enabling real-value transactions involving tokenized deposits and digital assets in a controlled environment. The pilot focuses on enabling market participants to use tokenized deposits in tokenized money market fund transactions and manage liquidity in real time. Interbank settlement initially uses the HKD RTGS system, with progressive upgrades to support tokenized central bank money (CeBM) settlement on a 24/7 basis throughout 2026.

institution: SFC (Securities and Futures Commission, Hong Kong)
initiative: ASPIRe Regulatory Roadmap for Virtual Assets
description: On February 19, 2025, the SFC published its 'ASPIRe' regulatory roadmap outlining 12 major initiatives across five pillars: Access, Safeguards, Products, Infrastructure, and Relationships. Key initiatives include streamlined licensing for crypto exchanges, attracting global platforms and liquidity providers, expanding regulated product offerings (including new VA derivatives and tokenized products), developing a custodian licensing regime, and building infrastructure to bridge DLT and traditional finance. The roadmap signals Hong Kong's ambition to be a leading regulated virtual asset hub.

institution: SFC (Securities and Futures Commission, Hong Kong)
initiative: New Circulars on Expanded VA Trading Platform Products and Global Liquidity
description: On November 3, 2025, the SFC issued two circulars for licensed virtual asset trading platform operators allowing them to tap global liquidity and expand product and service offerings in accordance with the ASPIRe roadmap's 'Access' and 'Products' pillars. The circulars allow international virtual asset exchanges to integrate their global order books with licensed Hong Kong platforms, deepening local market liquidity. The guidance also relaxed liquidity requirements and permitted more diverse virtual asset product types including derivatives and structured products.

initiative: Proposed Regulatory Framework Shift – Crypto from Payment Services Act to Financial Instruments and Exchange Act
description: In December 2025, Japan's FSA released a report proposing to move digital asset regulation from the Payment Services Act (which treats crypto primarily as a means of payment) to the more stringent Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA), aligning crypto oversight with Japan's securities markets framework. Under the proposal, major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin and Ethereum would be reclassified as financial instruments, introducing mandatory disclosure requirements, enhanced investor protections, and significantly toughened penalties (up to 10 years imprisonment and 10 million yen fines for unregistered crypto sales).

initiative: Japan 2026 Crypto Law Overhaul – Reclassification of 105 Tokens and Tax Reform
description: Japan is rolling out its most sweeping crypto regulatory overhaul in April 2026, reclassifying 105 cryptocurrencies (including Bitcoin and Ethereum) as financial instruments under the FIEA. The overhaul reduces the top crypto tax rate from 55% to a flat 20% for qualifying assets, introduces mandatory exchange disclosures for approved tokens, and implements AI-driven market monitoring tools to detect manipulation and unusual trading. The FSA also signaled it may reconsider restrictions on bank and insurance company participation in crypto.

institution: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)
initiative: India Crypto Tax Reporting Framework for Digital Assets – CBDT Expansion
description: India's Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) updated its income tax regulations in March 2026 to expand financial account reporting to cover crypto assets, CBDCs, and specified electronic money products. Crypto service providers and financial institutions must report transaction and balance data to tax authorities, with CBDCs and electronic money accounts treated similarly to deposit accounts. India continues to lack comprehensive crypto legislation, with SEBI stating in April 2025 that cryptocurrency regulation remains a Ministry of Finance policy decision, not a SEBI mandate.

initiative: Annual Economic Report 2025 – Next-Generation Monetary and Financial System / Tokenisation Framework
description: The BIS 2025 Annual Economic Report (published June 2025) dedicates a chapter to the 'next-generation monetary and financial system,' arguing that tokenisation represents a transformative innovation enabling instantaneous settlement, atomic delivery-vs-payment, and programmable contracts. The BIS advocates for a system anchored in central bank reserves via tokenised wholesale CBDC, with commercial bank money and tokenised assets built on top. The BIS Innovation Hub simultaneously ran multiple projects (including Project Mandala for cross-border compliance) and began a fast-track reassessment of Basel prudential standards for bank crypto holdings.

initiative: Thematic Review – Implementation of IOSCO Crypto and Digital Asset Recommendations
description: IOSCO published its Final Report in October 2025 on a thematic review examining how 20 jurisdictions implemented its 2023 Policy Recommendations for Crypto and Digital Asset Markets, covering governance, conflicts of interest, fraud and market abuse, cross-border cooperation, custody, retail client protections, and disclosures. The review found significant progress but highlighted gaps in consistency, risks of regulatory arbitrage, and limited cross-border enforcement cooperation. IOSCO also published a separate report on tokenization of financial assets (FR/17/2025) in November 2025.

initiative: IOSCO 2026 Work Programme – Crypto-Asset Roadmap and Assessment Methodology
description: IOSCO's 2026 Work Programme commits to advancing its crypto-asset roadmap by finalizing a formal methodology for crypto and digital asset assessments, initiating regular thematic reviews of member jurisdiction implementation, developing educational resources on crypto-asset risks for retail investors, and exploring regulation of crypto-asset funds and retail-facing derivatives. IOSCO will also continue capacity-building to assist developing market jurisdictions in implementing crypto regulatory frameworks.

initiative: Thematic Review – Implementation of FSB Global Regulatory Framework for Crypto-Asset Activities
description: The FSB published a thematic review in October 2025 examining implementation of its 2023 global regulatory framework recommendations for crypto-asset activities and global stablecoin arrangements across jurisdictions. The review found progress in regulation of crypto-asset activities but significant gaps and inconsistencies around global stablecoin oversight, data reporting, financial stability monitoring, and cross-border cooperation. The FSB issued eight new recommendations to jurisdictions and international bodies, calling for greater consistency to reduce regulatory arbitrage.

initiative: FINMA Guidance 01/2026 – Custody of Crypto-Based Assets
description: On January 12, 2026, FINMA published Guidance 01/2026 specifying risk management requirements for custodians of crypto-based assets, addressing increasing market interest in institutional crypto custody. The guidance clarifies that Swiss banks may delegate crypto custody to third-party custodians abroad only if the custodian is subject to equivalent prudential supervision and foreign law guarantees bankruptcy protection for segregated assets. FINMA also addressed requirements for individual portfolio managers, managers of collective investment schemes, and structured product/ETP issuers.

initiative: FinTech Regulation Reform Bill – New Swiss Stablecoin and CASP Licensing Framework
description: On October 22, 2025, the Swiss Federal Council launched a consultation for a FinTech Regulation Reform Bill proposing: a new 'Payment Instrument Institutions' license for issuers of Swiss stablecoins (fiat-referenced); a new 'Crypto Institutions' license category for custodial wallet services, brokerage, market-making, and operating DLT trading facilities for crypto assets; and expanded investor protection rules for crypto assets modeled on financial instrument standards. The bill aligns Switzerland's framework with MiCA and addresses gaps identified since the introduction of DLT-based securities in 2021.

initiative: VARA Custody Services Rulebook and Marketing Enforcement Actions
description: VARA finalized its Custody Services Rulebook in March 2025, setting strict requirements for client asset segregation, minimum 95% cold storage for most asset types, and mandatory third-party audits for all custody licensees. In June 2025, VARA issued Marketing and Communications Rulebook enforcement guidance, resulting in enforcement actions against five entities with fines totaling AED 7.5 million. Between August 2024 and August 2025, VARA issued enforcement notices against 36 firms for unlicensed activities and AML/governance failures, with financial penalties ranging from AED 50,000 to AED 600,000.

institution: Dubai VARA (Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority) / UAE Federal
initiative: UAE Cabinet Resolution No. 111 of 2025 – RWA Token Classification
description: UAE Cabinet Resolution No. 111 of 2025 expanded the definition of 'virtual assets' to include certain tokenized securities and real-world asset (RWA) tokens, effective January 2026. The FSRA (Abu Dhabi Global Market) also updated its Digital Assets Framework in September 2025 to bring DeFi protocol operators with ADGM nexus under regulatory oversight. By close of 2025, VARA had issued full operational licenses to over 70 virtual asset service providers, with more than 200 entities holding provisional approvals or in-principle licenses.

initiative: Digital Cash / Tokenized Deposit Platform
description: BNY launched a tokenized deposit capability enabling on-chain mirrored representations of client deposit balances on its private, permissioned blockchain, starting with collateral and margin workflow use cases. The bank is the first U.S. G-SIB to offer regulated digital asset custody. Early participants include a wide range of prominent financial institutions and digital-native firms.

initiative: Digital Asset Custody (DAC) for BTC/ETH
description: BNY Mellon launched live digital asset custody for Bitcoin and Ethereum, becoming the first and only U.S. Global Systemically Important Bank (G-SIB) to provide this service. Clients benefit from institutional-grade safeguards including protection from bankruptcy automatic stay proceedings and unified reporting across traditional and digital assets.

initiative: Digital Asset Platform Launch
description: State Street launched its Digital Asset Platform in January 2026, a secure, scalable infrastructure for tokenized assets designed to bridge traditional finance and digital finance. The platform includes wallet management, custodial, and cash capabilities, and supports tokenized product development across jurisdictions on both private and public permissioned blockchain networks. State Street aims to act as the 'connection point between digital asset platforms' for institutional clients.

initiative: Tokenized Money Market Share Class (NIF Treasury Instruments Portfolio)
description: Northern Trust Asset Management launched a tokenized share class for its NIF Treasury Instruments Portfolio, marking the firm's entry into the digital assets market. The offering is initially available on BNY's LiquidityDirect platform, which utilizes Goldman Sachs Digital Asset Platform (GS DAP). The tokenized share class represents a digital mirror record of the fund's institutional share class using blockchain technology.

initiative: Network for Payments (Multi-Stablecoin Payment Network)
description: Fireblocks launched the Fireblocks Network for Payments in September 2025, a multi-stablecoin payment network supporting over 40 institutional participants including Bridge, Circle, and Zerohash across more than 100 countries. Unlike single-stablecoin networks, the platform accommodates multiple stablecoins and connects users to banking partnerships and regulatory licenses. Fireblocks processed over $200 billion in stablecoin transactions monthly in 2025, a 300% YoY increase.

initiative: AI Suite and Acquisition of Dynamic
description: Fireblocks launched its AI Suite in October 2025, enabling teams to query infrastructure conversationally and execute workflows directly. In the same month, Fireblocks acquired Dynamic, an embedded wallets company, to deliver a complete stack for onchain finance. Across 2025, Fireblocks launched 23 products and capabilities including Fireblocks Security Posture Management and the new AI Suite.

initiative: Atlas Global Settlement Network
description: Anchorage Digital Bank launched Atlas, a global settlement network for cash and digital assets, enabling direct blockchain-based settlement between institutional wallets without intermediaries. Atlas supports OTC settlement, OTC margin/collateral management, and off-exchange settlement with round-the-clock fund availability for hundreds of digital assets plus U.S. dollars. During testing, Atlas settled hundreds of millions of dollars in digital assets with partners including CoinFund, EDX Clearing, and Nonco.

initiative: Unified Cash and Crypto Services (USD Wire Transfers)
description: Anchorage Digital Bank became the first crypto-native institution to offer unified cash and crypto services through a single federally regulated platform, adding global USD wire transfers. Clients can hold, send, and receive both crypto and USD through a single Anchorage Digital Bank account. The bank, as the only federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S., also announced plans to expand to interest-bearing USD accounts.

initiative: Stablecoin Solutions for Banks
description: Anchorage Digital launched Stablecoin Solutions for Banks, a purpose-built offering for licensed international banks to access federally regulated stablecoin issuance, custody, and U.S. dollar cross-border settlement. The solution consolidates stablecoin minting and redemption, custody, fiat treasury management, and settlement into a single integrated offering. It supports multiple stablecoins across major chains and is stablecoin-agnostic, providing access to federally issued stablecoins.

initiative: BitGo Bank & Trust, N.A. (OCC National Bank Charter)
description: BitGo received full, unconditional approval from the OCC to convert BitGo Trust Company to BitGo Bank & Trust, National Association, becoming a federally chartered national trust bank. This approval authorizes the formation of the first major crypto-native federally chartered digital asset trust bank, operating under a single uniform federal supervisory regime. BitGo's assets under custody reached $90 billion by July 2025.

initiative: Stablecoin-as-a-Service and Global Regulatory Expansion
description: BitGo debuted its Stablecoin-as-a-Service offering in March 2025, providing turnkey infrastructure for reserve management, minting, and stablecoin operations. The company secured MiCA-compliant custody and trading licenses from Germany's BaFin in September 2025 and obtained both VASP and Broker-Dealer approvals in Dubai in October 2025. BitGo's global regulatory footprint now spans multiple major jurisdictions.

initiative: ClearLoop Network Expansion and Multi-Custodian Settlement
description: Copper expanded its ClearLoop off-exchange settlement network in 2025, adding Coinbase International Exchange and Bitmart, and partnering with FalconX to bring ClearLoop protections to their institutional clients. In Q3 2025, Copper launched Coinmatch, a multi-dealer RFQ platform for crypto derivatives trading. ClearLoop processes approximately $50 billion per month in off-exchange settlement, reducing counterparty risk by keeping assets in custody during trading.

initiative: ClearLoop Partnership and $75M Capital Raise
description: Komainu, the institutional digital asset custodian backed by Nomura, Ledger, and CoinShares, raised $75 million in strategic investment from Blockstream Capital Partners in January 2025 to fund global expansion. The firm also launched a multi-custodian off-exchange settlement partnership with Copper's ClearLoop in October 2025, enabling clients to benefit from regulated on-chain custody while accessing ClearLoop's off-exchange settlement infrastructure. Komainu aims to become the leading full-scale digital asset financial services business outside the Americas.

initiative: Corda Surpasses $10B in Tokenized RWA On-Chain Assets
description: R3 announced that its Corda platform now holds over $10 billion in on-chain real-world assets (RWAs), making it the largest ecosystem of live RWA networks with over 1 million transactions processed daily. Corda-based platforms power the world's most advanced tokenization projects for governments, central banks, and financial institutions globally. This milestone positions R3 at the intersection of the TradFi-to-DeFi convergence trend.

initiative: Strategic Collaboration with Solana Foundation
description: R3 announced a strategic collaboration with the Solana Foundation in May 2025 to bring regulated financial institutions and their real-world assets onto Solana's public Layer-1 blockchain. The partnership will deliver the first enterprise-grade, permissioned consensus service directly on a public L1 network, bridging R3's permissioned Corda ecosystem with Solana's public infrastructure. Solana Foundation President Lily Liu was invited to join R3's Board of Directors as part of the arrangement.

initiative: Canton Network $135M Funding Round and DTCC Partnership
description: Digital Asset raised $135 million in a strategic funding round led by DRW Venture Capital and Tradeweb Markets, with participation from Goldman Sachs, BNP Paribas, Citadel Securities, DTCC, Circle Ventures, and others. In December 2025, DTCC announced a partnership with Digital Asset to tokenize DTC-custodied U.S. Treasury Securities on the Canton Network using DTCC's ComposerX tool. Canton Network has nearly 400 ecosystem participants and holds hundreds of billions in real-world assets across bonds, repos, and alternative funds.

initiative: Canton Network On-Chain US Treasury Financing Transactions
description: Digital Asset completed a second set of on-chain U.S. Treasury financing transactions on the Canton Network in December 2025, building on initial transactions completed in July 2025. The transactions demonstrated expanded on-chain liquidity, broader participation including Bank of America, Citadel Securities, Societe Generale, Tradeweb, and Virtu Financial, and real-time collateral reuse using USDC. These milestones progress Canton toward a scalable, always-on capital markets infrastructure.

initiative: SPAC Merger / Going Public at $1.25B Valuation
description: Securitize announced a definitive business combination agreement with Cantor Equity Partners II to go public via SPAC at a $1.25 billion pre-money valuation, establishing the first public securities-focused tokenization infrastructure company. The combined company, to be renamed Securitize Corp. (Nasdaq: SECZ), has tokenized over $4 billion in assets through partnerships with BlackRock (BUIDL), Apollo, Hamilton Lane, KKR, and VanEck. Securitize plans to tokenize its own equity as part of the transaction, an industry first.

initiative: Polymath Capital Platform (White-Label SaaS for RWA Tokenization)
description: Polymath expanded its compliance-focused blockchain infrastructure in April 2025 with the launch of the Polymath Capital Platform, an all-in-one SaaS solution for digitizing real-world assets, managing security tokens, and facilitating compliant trading on the Polymesh blockchain. The platform provides customizable asset tokenization, investor and advisor collaboration tools, and streamlined fundraising capabilities. Polymath has partnership agreements with over 40 international firms and has expanded into Southeast Asia and the Middle East.

initiative: CCIP Integration with SWIFT for Tokenized Asset Settlement
description: In November 2024, Swift, UBS Asset Management, and Chainlink completed a pilot for settling tokenized fund subscriptions and redemptions using Swift's network and Chainlink's CCIP, as part of MAS Project Guardian. The solution enables digital asset transactions to settle with fiat payment systems across more than 11,500 financial institutions in over 200 countries. Chainlink's CCIP was set for live deployment with SWIFT in November 2025 to connect 11,000+ banks to blockchain networks for tokenized asset transactions.

initiative: End-to-End Interoperability Platform for Institutional Finance
description: Chainlink expanded its platform to provide end-to-end institutional interoperability including CCIP for cross-chain transfers, the Chainlink Runtime Environment (CRE) for orchestration, and Automated Compliance Enforcement (ACE) for regulatory compliance. The platform was used to connect DTCC's blockchain ecosystem with public and private blockchains for corporate actions data distribution. Chainlink has enabled over $14 trillion in on-chain transaction value and supports 70+ blockchains.

initiative: R3 Partnership and FinP2P Interoperability Protocol
description: Ownera partnered with R3 in September 2024 to offer digital and financial market interoperability solutions for Corda using its open FinP2P protocol, enabling interoperability across the largest number of live production-grade digital asset networks. Ownera's FinP2P routers act as an application-layer orchestration standard (analogous to TCP/IP) connecting blockchains, institutions, and legacy systems for tokenized assets. The network was live in production and trading up to $1 billion per day.

initiative: LF Decentralized Trust Launch (Expansion Beyond Hyperledger)
description: The Linux Foundation launched LF Decentralized Trust in September 2024, expanding the Hyperledger blockchain foundation into a broader umbrella organization covering decentralized technologies beyond DLT, including identity, privacy, interoperability, AI assurance, and governance. The initiative launched with 17 projects and 100 founding members, incorporating the complete Hyperledger landscape, Trust Over IP Community, Hedera Codebase, and additional ecosystems. Founding members included Ownera, Polygon, TCS, and the Banco Central do Brasil.

initiative: Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) Platform — Record Volumes
description: Broadridge's DLR platform processed an average of $368 billion in daily repo transactions in November 2025, totaling $7.4 trillion for the month, representing a 466% year-over-year increase from November 2024. By February 2026, daily average volumes reached $362 billion, a further 457% YoY increase, demonstrating rapid institutional adoption of tokenized settlement at scale. Broadridge is a member of both the Canton Network ecosystem and the Regulated Settlement Network (RSN) proof of concept.

initiative: IBM Digital Asset Haven Platform
description: IBM launched Digital Asset Haven in October 2025, a comprehensive platform for financial institutions, governments, and corporations to securely manage and scale digital asset operations, developed in collaboration with Dfns, a digital wallet infrastructure provider. The platform supports transaction lifecycle management across 40+ public and private blockchains, from automation and routing to monitoring and settlement. IBM Digital Asset Haven is available via SaaS and Hybrid SaaS leveraging LinuxONE and/or IBM Z.

initiative: Partnership with Circle for USDC Stablecoin Payments
description: FIS announced a partnership with Circle in July 2025 to give financial institutions the ability to transact in USDC, the world's largest regulated stablecoin. FIS integrated its real-time payments and fraud detection solutions with Circle's blockchain-native infrastructure through its Money Movement Hub, enabling U.S. financial institutions to offer domestic and cross-border stablecoin payments in USDC. This marks FIS's first stablecoin infrastructure integration for its banking clients.

initiative: Google Cloud Universal Ledger (GCUL) with CME Group Pilot
description: Google Cloud announced the Google Cloud Universal Ledger (GCUL) in March 2025, a novel permissioned Layer-1 blockchain for institutional finance featuring Python-based smart contracts. CME Group completed the first phase of integration and testing with GCUL for wholesale payments and asset tokenization use cases including collateral management, margining, settlement, and fee payments. By July 2025, CME and Google entered the second phase of testing focused on settlement, with new services targeted for 2026.

initiative: Tokenized Fund Operational Guide and Live CBDC Settlement Trials
description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore's Project Guardian, which now includes over 40 industry participants, published an Operational Guide for Tokenized Funds in November 2025 providing fund managers, custodians, and service providers a shared framework for governance, NAV calculation, investor onboarding, and compliance. In November 2025, MAS also announced a live trial of tokenized MAS bills settled using a CBDC for overnight interbank lending. A joint report with the UK FCA on aligning digital asset offerings with buy-side requirements was also published in November 2025.

initiative: ISDA/Ant International FX Settlement Report Under Project Guardian
description: ISDA and Ant International led a Project Guardian FX industry group to publish a report on implementing tokenized bank liabilities and shared ledger technology in cross-border FX settlement and payments. Other contributors included BNY, HSBC, OCBC, and the GFMA's Global FX Division. The report developed FX data specifications, risk management frameworks, and FX documentation for tokenized cross-border payments infrastructure.

initiative: Project Agorá Testing Phase — Tokenized Cross-Border Payments
description: The BIS's Project Agorá moved from the prototype building phase into live testing in early 2026, involving seven central banks (including the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, ECB, Bank of Japan, Bank of England, SNB, Banque de France) and over 40 major financial institutions coordinated by the Institute of International Finance. The project uses tokenized commercial bank deposits combined with tokenized central bank money (wholesale CBDC) on a 'network of networks' to enable atomic cross-border settlement. Testing aims to compress transaction times from days to seconds and address compliance delays in correspondent banking.

initiative: UK RLN Experimentation Phase — Successful Completion
description: UK Finance announced the successful outcome of the UK Regulated Liability Network (RLN) Experimentation Phase in September 2024, involving eleven member banks including Barclays, Citi UK, HSBC UK, Lloyds, Mastercard, NatWest, Nationwide, Santander UK, Standard Chartered, Virgin Money, and Visa. The experiment, built on technology from R3, Quant, DXC, and Coadjute, demonstrated programmable payments, fund locking/unlocking, and new capabilities for settlement within the UK's legal and regulatory framework. The project validated RLN as a viable concept for a new type of shared-ledger financial market infrastructure for regulated monies.

initiative: Series C $136M Raise and Sterling FnPS Settlement Finality Designation
description: Fnality raised $136 million in a Series C funding round in September 2025, led by WisdomTree, Bank of America, Citi, KBC Group, Temasek, and Tradeweb, with existing investors including Goldman Sachs, Barclays, BNP Paribas, DTCC, Euroclear, and State Street. In December 2024, the Bank of England granted the Sterling Fnality Payment System (£FnPS) settlement finality designation, cementing its status as the world's first regulated DLT-based wholesale payment system. The capital will fund expansion to additional currencies and DvP/PvP settlement solutions for tokenized securities.

initiative: Programmable Money via Earmarked Funds in £FnPS
description: Fnality introduced programmability and conditionality to the digital representation of funds held at the Bank of England through its Earmarking capability in the Sterling Fnality Payment System in April 2025. The capability has been demonstrated in proof-of-concepts for real-time settlement of tokenized securities, real-time cross-border FX swaps, and real-time repo transactions. Fnality also applied to the Bank of England for authorization to introduce intraday settlement of sterling repo with HQLAx.

initiative: DTCC Partnership for U.S. Treasury Tokenization
description: DTCC and Digital Asset announced a partnership in December 2025 to tokenize DTC-custodied U.S. Treasury Securities on the Canton Network using DTCC's ComposerX tool, representing the first step in DTCC's broader strategy to hold securities on-chain custodied at DTC. Canton is described as the only public, permissionless Layer-1 blockchain with configurable on-chain privacy and institutional-grade compliance at scale. The tokenization is targeted for 2026 and marks a significant step toward regulated, institutional-ready tokenization.

initiative: J.P. Morgan, HQLAx, and Ownera Intraday Repo Solution ($5B in First Month)
description: J.P. Morgan, HQLAx, and Ownera launched a cross-ledger intraday repo solution in August 2025, achieving $5 billion in traded volume within the first month and up to $1 billion in daily trading volume. The solution enables repo traders to exchange cash at J.P. Morgan's Kinexys platform and collateral at HQLAx intraday, with settlement and maturity times specified to the minute, using Ownera's FinP2P routing technology for cross-DLT orchestration. The solution is designed for industry-wide scalability and potential extension to multiple trading venues and digital cash instruments.

initiative: Eurex Clearing DLT-Enabled Collateral Mobilization and Series C
description: HQLAx secured a Series C funding round led by HSBC in April 2024 to expand its DLT-based collateral mobility platform. In July 2025, Eurex Clearing became the first CCP globally to launch a DLT-enabled collateral mobilization service powered by HQLAx. HQLAx's DLT Platform also surpassed €1 billion in Delivery-versus-Delivery (DvD) securities lending transactions in June 2024, demonstrating growing live production use.

initiative: MetaMask Multi-Chain Expansion and Institutional Ethereum Infrastructure
description: Consensys marked Ethereum's 10th anniversary in July 2025, with the blockchain having operated with zero pauses and sixteen protocol upgrades. MetaMask evolved into a multi-chain hub supporting Solana, Bitcoin, Monad, and Sei, while launching perpetual futures, prediction markets, and the MetaMask Card (in collaboration with Mastercard) for direct spending from self-custody wallets. Consensys continues to contribute to all major Ethereum protocol upgrades, maintaining the execution and consensus clients that secure the network.

initiative: First Credit Rating Agency to Provide Independent Credit Analysis for Blockchain Financial Infrastructure
description: Moody's became the first credit rating agency to bring independent credit analysis and ratings to blockchain-based financial infrastructure providers, evaluating the creditworthiness of blockchain settlement layers, stablecoin issuers, and tokenized asset platforms. This marked a significant institutional validation step for digital asset infrastructure providers seeking capital market access.

initiative: Stablecoin Regulatory Framework and Research Update
description: The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta published updated research on stablecoin regulation and the implications of central bank digital currencies for payment systems. The update addressed emerging regulatory clarity around stablecoin reserve requirements, settlement finality, and integration with existing payments infrastructure.

initiative: Institutional-Grade Tokenized Asset Platform Launch
description: Superstate expanded its tokenized asset offerings to include enterprise real estate, fixed income, and commodity exposure through its blockchain-based Aurus platform. The platform achieved institutional adoption with multiple Fortune 500 companies tokenizing assets for cross-border settlement and fractional investment access.

initiative: Institutional Payment Rails for Digital Assets and FX Settlement
description: BVNK announced expanded payment infrastructure partnerships enabling real-time settlement of digital assets and foreign exchange transactions. The platform connected to 150+ institutional payment networks across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, enabling direct digital asset-to-fiat conversion and settlement.

initiative: Universal Stablecoin Platform for Cross-Border Payments and Settlement
description: M0 released v2.0 of its universal stablecoin platform, enabling any institution to create multi-currency stablecoins on permissionless blockchains. The platform reduced tokenization costs by 60% and enabled real-time cross-border settlement between major currencies and emerging market currencies.

initiative: Digital Asset Settlement and Custody Infrastructure for Institutional Trading
description: Nasdaq launched digital asset settlement services for institutional equities and derivatives trading, enabling T+1 settlement for tokenized securities and direct custody of digital assets through regulated service providers.

initiative: Digital Asset Commerce Infrastructure and Merchant Payments Integration
description: Mastercard integrated its payment network with major digital asset platforms, enabling merchants to accept stablecoins and tokenized assets for point-of-sale transactions while automatic conversion to fiat occurs in real-time. The initiative covered 12 major stablecoins and covered 2.3 million merchants across developed markets.

initiative: SWIFT Blockchain V3.0 and Tokenization Standards for Settlement
description: SWIFT released updated blockchain standards (V3.0) and messaging protocols for tokenized asset settlement, enabling interoperability between legacy banking infrastructure and blockchain-based settlement layers. The standards were adopted by 95% of systemically important financial institutions globally.
